- [Serving engines](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/supported/engines.md) ŌĆö Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and the in-kernel engine.

## Measuring sticks (the scorecards)

Each turns a fuzzy goal into a number you can drive toward zero.

- [Industry scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/industry-scorecard/README.md) ŌĆö the one OUTWARD stick: an industry-first taxonomy of the dimensions the LLM-serving field competes on (vLLM/SGLang/TensorRT-LLM/llama.cpp), with fak's honest position on each (mostly named gaps). Two driven numbers: **coverage** (of the field) and **parity-debt** (honesty of the rows). Modular folder, regenerated from `tools/industry_scorecard.data/`.
- [Agent-readiness scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/AGENT-READINESS-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö can an AI agent discover, adopt, and build on fak (friction-debt across the three steps an agent walks).
- [Maturity scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/MATURITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the one LIFECYCLE stick: where each declared capability (one per `internal/<leaf>` lane) sits on a closed ladder (`proposedŌåÆprototypedŌåÆtestedŌåÆdogfoodedŌåÆdefault`, + a `benchmarked` badge), and the next work item that would mature it. Immaturity is never a defect ŌĆö only a **ladder-skip** (fak relies on a capability yet leaves it untested) is. `fak maturity next` is the ranked backlog the dispatch loop pulls from; re-derived from `dos.toml` + the tree's import graph.
- [Repo-hygiene scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/REPO-HYGIENE-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö verbosity, organization, indexing, and accessibility of the whole tree.
- [Code-quality scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/CODE-QUALITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the Go module's code-debt.
- [Testing and linting infrastructure scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/TESTING-LINTING-INFRA-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö current-state score plus a top-20 spine for making fak's tests, linting, CI, and agentic developer loop faster, better typed, more modular, and easier to trust.
- [Steerability scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/STEERABILITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the one GROWTH-INVARIANT stick: does the effort to steer/change/navigate the repo stay flat as it grows? A 0ŌĆō100 **steerability index** over modularity, coupling (blast radius), navigability, and correction ŌĆö every KPI a ratio/percentile, so a 2├Ś repo with the same discipline scores the same. The number every count-based sibling can't give.
- [Observability scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/OBSERVABILITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the observability plane (metrics, dashboards, alerts, traces, proofs, ship-audit), graded so every dashboard/alert/doc points at a metric the binary emits and every claim is verifiable.
- [Conflation scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/CONFLATION-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the truth-maintenance stick: does every number/status fak reports label its provenance ŌĆö WITNESSED (a fact fak authored, in its control) vs OBSERVED (a value fak relays from an external party) ŌĆö and never blame a provider-side miss on a fak action? Reads the fact-reporting surfaces (metric help, the guard exit summary) so a dashboard can't say "fak broke the cache" when the provider's cache simply expired.
- [Default-value scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/DEFAULT-VALUE-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the recurring guard for epic #1089: does every cost/cache/amplification value-flag in `cmd/fak/{guard,serve}.go` ship default-ON (or gated OFF with a documented reason), does no exit-summary line fold `kernel.Counters` that are structurally 0 on the Decide-only `fak guard -- claude` proxy (VACUOUS_ON_GUARD), and does no score surface default its headline to a modeled "planned" number (C_MODELED_NOT_OBSERVED)? Parses the flag definitions and exit/score surfaces from the Go source so "a value feature shipped not-fully-enabled" becomes a tracked `default_value_debt` integer, not a one-off chat. Worst-first retirement maps onto #1090-#1095.
- [UI/UX-quality scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/UI-QUALITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the one TERMINAL-SURFACE stick: do the `fak console` panes, the `fak info` overlay, and `fak guard --split` render correctly and legibly? Graded against the render source itself ŌĆö rune-safe truncation (no byte-slice that splits a multibyte rune), cell-aware column pads (no `%-Ns` shear on a multibyte row), empty-state branches, full info-legend and console-help coverage ŌĆö folded into a `ui_quality_debt` integer. The first ship retired the `trimTUI` byte-slice that emitted a half rune on any em-dash/emoji.
- [Demo-quality scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/DEMO-QUALITY-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö are the demos runnable, honest, self-contained.
- [Demo-robustness scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/DEMO-ROBUSTNESS-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö do the demos survive bad input and odd environments.
- [Learning-docs scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/LEARNING-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö does the teaching set actually teach (learning-debt: a how-to with no runnable command, a tutorial with no worked output, an orphan lesson, an uncovered topic). Pedagogy counterpart of repo-hygiene (structure) and doc-appeal (voice).
- [Concept-usage scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/fak/concept-usage-scorecard.md) ŌĆö the INWARD dogfooding stick: when an agent BUILDS fak, how much does that development route through fak's *own* concepts? Two axes, both re-derived from `git log` + the `.dos` journals ŌĆö **usage** (the ship-stamp / DCO / binding-verb commit discipline + lane arbitration) and **witness** (the `verify`/`improve` syscalls over passive recall). The thin axis is witness: it catches development that ships with fak's commit clothes but still trusts a self-report instead of witnessing its own claim. Breadth-and-depth sibling of the [dogfood-loop scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/fak/dogfood-loop-scorecard.md) (one launched-session honesty loop).
- [Loop scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/fak/loop-scorecard.md) ŌĆö are fak's always-on agentic background loops *first-class durable processes*, or fire-and-forget scripts? Three axes, all re-derived from the loop ledger + job registry `fak loop` writes ŌĆö **durability** (auto-restart on system restart: every firing loop registered, armed, and `fak cron emit`-projectable to an OS unit), **self-report** (no dark loop; fires that record an end outcome; heartbeat/notify), and **dogfood** (runs route through `fak loop run` under `fak guard`, the canonical hash-chained ledger, the witness contract). The honest baseline is debt 4 / composite 33 (F): the loops that fire are driven by external schedulers, so they vanish on reboot, go dark, and run unguarded ŌĆö the 3├Ś program drives them through `fak loop run`. Operational sibling of the [concept-usage scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/fak/concept-usage-scorecard.md).
- [Release-readiness scorecard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/RELEASE-READINESS-SCORECARD.md) ŌĆö the RELEASE-VELOCITY stick: can a kernel that writes hundreds of commits a day cut, validate, publish, and roll back a release at the same speed, or does `@latest` rot far behind HEAD? KPIs across the release lifecycle ŌĆö **discover** (an agent can find & invoke the path), **automate** (the machine cuts on green, not a human), **validate** (the cut is gated and the publish verified), **trust** (stable anchors, signed artifacts, rollback) ŌĆö folded into a `release-debt` integer, all re-derived from git + the tracked tree + live release signals. Honest baseline is debt 12 / composite 16 (F): the cut is a hand-driven 7-step ritual and the cadence is dry-run-only, so `@latest` sits ~1900 commits behind HEAD; the 3├Ś program (epic #1354) automates the cut and makes the path agent-discoverable.
- [Cadence report](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/cadence/README.md) ŌĆö the regular control-pane fold over scores, feature maturity, work done, and release state. Its append-only ledger records `standing_score`, normalized health, and difficulty fields so trends can keep climbing or fall instead of treating a bounded 100 as a stable meaning after the scorecard surface changes.
- [Milestone status](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/milestones/STATUS.md) ŌĆö the durable, freshness-checked snapshot of the maturity CLIMB: the model x backend grid's distribution across the closed M0ŌĆōM7 support-maturity ladder, generated from `internal/covmatrix` + `internal/milestonereport` and kept honest by `fak milestone status-doc --check-doc` (a committed cell cannot silently drift from the live grid). The `gh`-fed epic ROADMAP stays on the Slack card + the durable `docs/milestones/history.jsonl` trend ledger.
- [Code-2x program](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/CODE-2X-PROGRAM.md) ŌĆö the plan to halve code-debt, then halve it again.

## Architecture & design

- [Architecture](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md) ŌĆö the registry seams and the frozen ABI.
- [GPU forward pass](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/GPU.md) ŌĆö the in-kernel Llama decode on the GPU: a real on-box run witnessed against the CPU reference, with the honest gap to llama.cpp.
- [Partitioning](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/PARTITION.md) ŌĆö how the kernel splits work across lanes and leaves.
- [Extending fak](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/EXTENDING.md) ŌĆö plug in an optimization, prove it correct, prove it faster.
- [SOTA comparison](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/SOTA-COMPARISON.md) ŌĆö where fak sits next to the state of the art.
- [The cross-platform spine (IoT to hyperscaler)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/explainers/cross-platform-spine.md) ŌĆö why the same pure-Go kernel is the invariant spine across the whole deployment spectrum (IoT, edge, laptop, hyperscaler), the way Linux is one kernel under a phone and a datacenter: the hardware specifics change, the agentic workload shape and the kernel's invariants do not. The deployment-substrate axis, alongside the scale axis ([loops](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/explainers/engineering-is-building-loops.md)) and the hardware-depth axis ([HAL](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/explainers/hardware-portability.md)).
- [Interoperability stance](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/integrations/interoperability.md) ŌĆö why fak adopts whatever agent/model/framework you run (the one opinion kept is the floor) + the honest per-wire grade for the flagship harnesses and every interop protocol.
- [Agent-to-agent value](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/a2a-value-opportunities.md) ŌĆö where the cross-agent cache pays off.
- [Multi-agent coordination protocol (RFC, D-007)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/multi-agent-coordination-protocol.md) ŌĆö the normative spec binding message passing (a2achan), shared state (sharedtask), and coordination primitives (comm) under one default-deny floor.
- [Shared state ladder](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/shared-state-ladder.md) ŌĆö the canonical split for shared live messages, live mutable state, durable handoff, disaggregated state, and user-level collaborative editing.
- [Shared task record contract](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/shared-task-record-contract.md) ŌĆö the executable envelope contract for collaborative task records, user patches, conflicts, approval gates, and disaggregated artifact refs.
- [Task manager concept](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/task-manager.md) ŌĆö the process-local runtime snapshot for tasks, steps, resource usage, concept runtime, progress, and ETA.
- [AgentŌĆōmachine link protocol](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/agent-machine-link-protocol.md) ŌĆö how an agent and a host node bind.
- [Glossary](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/glossary.md) ŌĆö the canonical split for overloaded core words (session, agent, context, model, memory, tool/skill, steering), shared-memory senses, and the live memory issue-owner map.
- [DOS kernel transfer playbook](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/dos-kernel-transfer-playbook.md) ŌĆö moving the trust substrate to a new repo.
- [Prefill visuals](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/prefill-visuals.md) ŌĆö the diagrams behind prefill reuse.

## Operating the agent fleet

- [`fleet` operator console](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/tools/FLEET.md) ŌĆö one command to watch the agent fleet on a host: live session/account health, what stopped and why, which accounts are resumable, and what needs you. The session-health companion to `dos top`; install it onto PATH with `fleet install`.
- [Run it all night (`fak nightrun`)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/nightrun/README.md) ŌĆö the data-collection center of excellence: the one door an operator/agent uses to answer "what is the single most important datum I can collect on THIS box, right now?" then collect the feasible queue on a loop into a durable ledger.
- [GPU server overnight run plan (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/nightrun/DGX-OVERNIGHT-PLAN-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö the fleet-scale companion: per-box overnight data-collection plan for the GPU server/CPU server boxes reached over the Slack control bridge (where the frontier GLM-5.2 witnesses live), with the live fleet state, the exact runbook per box, and the honesty boundary.
- [Mac metal node overnight run plan (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/nightrun/MAC-OVERNIGHT-PLAN-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö the Apple-Silicon companion: the fak-kernel Qwen3.6-27B Q4_K decode witness collected on the Metal verify node (warm ~1.6ŌĆō1.9 tok/s, climbing with length), why `--context-budget-tokens` is load-bearing on the 36 GB box, and the resume conditions.

## Benchmarks & methodology

- [Benchmark authority](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md) ŌĆö the single source of truth for every number.
- [Benchmark template](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/BENCHMARK-TEMPLATE.md) ŌĆö the shape a new benchmark result doc must take.
- [Net-true value standard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/standards/net-true-value.md) ŌĆö how fak decides a gain is real, not noise: the six-question rubric (real baseline / net of cost / scope / provenance / witness / realized) used on fak's own claims and on incoming industry "5├Ś" claims, each criterion bound to the stick that enforces it.
- [Agent grammar standard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/standards/agent-grammar.md) ŌĆö the normative trust grammar a second agent fleet conforms to: the closed nouns (lane ┬Ę lease ┬Ę reason token ┬Ę witness ┬Ę verdict ┬Ę claim ┬Ę ladder rung ┬Ę scope), the shipped verbs each with an inputŌåÆverdict signature and the closed vocabulary it draws from (every verb maps to a `dos_*` MCP verb / `dos.toml` surface today), the lift recipe as MUST clauses, the `G6` one-sided-screen + witnessed-loss polarity predicate as a checkable MUST, and a per-verb conformance checklist ŌĆö the role `internal/abi`'s golden freeze plays for the ABI.
- [Observer-effect standard](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/standards/observer-effect.md) ŌĆö the cost-side companion: the perf-floor/security-floor duality, the WITNESSED/OBSERVED/MODELED/SIMULATED label required on every overhead number fak reports about itself, and the fence that a hot-path meter's own cost must be under a declared cap proven by a green test (the shipped `AcceptanceMeter` is pinned at 0 allocations/sample).
- [Support-maturity honesty fence](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/standards/support-maturity-honesty-fence.md) ŌĆö the support-side companion (epic #1243): the net-true-value lens on *support* claims. Three rules keep the maturity ladder (M0 none ŌåÆ M7 beyond-SOTA) from becoming a wish-list ŌĆö no self-reported promotion (a rung rises only on a non-author witness, the shipgate keep-bit), a rung can drop (the scorecard re-derives every rung from the live grid, so a stale/regressed witness demotes with no latch), and every rung carries a WITNESSED/OBSERVED/MODELED label (only WITNESSED attains a rung).
- [Hardware bench plan](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/bench-plan.md) ŌĆö auto-generated: the next highest-value test per bench-node (regenerate with `tools/bench_plan.py`, don't hand-edit).
- [Web-agent baselines](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/webbench-baselines.md) ŌĆö the modeled WebVoyager prefill geometry (vs the naive floor).
- [Web-agent blockers](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/webbench-blockers.md) ŌĆö what is not yet measured, and why.
- [Web-agent measurement summary](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/webbench-real-measurements-summary.md) ŌĆö the rolled-up real-run numbers.
- [DOS hook cost](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/perf-dos-hook-cost.md) ŌĆö what the commit-time guards cost in practice.
- [Runaway-guard cost](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/perf-runaway-guard.md) ŌĆö the cost of the process-reaper backstop.

## Status & tracking

Working docs that track a specific effort. Dated by design; they age out.

- [Executive roll-up](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/EXECUTIVE-ROLLUP.md) ŌĆö the leadership snapshot: flagship wins, the live GLM-5.2 goal, the real risks, and the one open decision ŌĆö every number provenance-labeled (witnessed / observed / simulated / unverified). Aggregated from PRODUCT-STATUS, BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY, the AgentDojo red-team, the dispatch audit, and the industry scorecard.
- [Issue tracker](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues) ŌĆö the live, always-current open-issue count. This index never hard-codes a count (it drifts); see the tracker for the current number.
- [Support-maturity disambiguation epic (#1243)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/support-maturity-disambiguation-tracking-1243.md) ŌĆö splits the one fuzzy word "supported" into a closed, witnessed level ladder (none ŌåÆ loads ŌåÆ runs ŌåÆ correct ŌåÆ optimized ŌåÆ SOTA-parity ŌåÆ beyond-SOTA) and a router that turns each cell's rung into the right dev-regime, time-horizon, and tooling ŌĆö so a 10-step "make it work" is never confused with a 10,000-step "make it fast". Extends `internal/covmatrix`; the measurement+routing instrument above the Parity tracks (#307/#305/#303/#301).
- [GPU parity tracking (#480)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/gpu-parity-tracking-480.md) ŌĆö bringing the GPU path to parity.
- [SIMD CPU parity tracking (#400)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/simd-cpu-parity-tracking-400.md) ŌĆö Go-native SIMD (AVX2/AVX-512/NEON) vs llama.cpp on CPU: parity where SIMD is the lever, non-SIMD residuals named.
- [Track B performance parity tracking (#306)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/track-b-performance-parity-tracking-306.md) ŌĆö roll-up status for the eight Track B children, with stale migrated issue links corrected and GPU-gated/unimplemented acceptance blockers kept explicit.
- [Track D agent-framework parity tracking (#304)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/track-d-agent-framework-parity-tracking-304.md) ŌĆö roll-up status for the eight Track D children, with stale migrated issue links corrected; D-001 benchmark scaffold (smoke green, published run bench-node-gated), D-002 closed by wire repoint, D-003/D-004 wire-supported with adapter acceptance open, D-005/D-006 unimplemented, D-007/D-008 scaffolds tracked by sibling epics.
- [The self-tax plane ŌĆö performance-assurance epic (#1147)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/self-tax-performance-assurance-tracking-1147.md) ŌĆö first-class, always-on evidence that fak's own gates/guards/verification cost no more than a declared budget (and naming it when they make work faster): the mediation-overhead dual of the security floor, mechanizing net-true-value Q2 (net-of-cost) across turn-by-turn ŌåÆ post-session ŌåÆ CI-gate ŌåÆ model-as-judge ŌåÆ observability. Sibling of #306 (mediation overhead vs raw-inference parity).

## Notes & research (`docs/notes/`)

Dated working notes: research, audits, and run logs. Kept for the record, not the
front page.

- [Self-feature query spine ŌĆö fak asks fak what it can do (2026-06-30)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SELF-FEATURE-QUERY-SPINE-2026-06-30.md) ŌĆö issue spine [#1484](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1484) for making fak's dev facts, live gateway tools, and memory strategies queryable to agents: cards first, detail/explain on demand, and every selected action routed through `fak_adjudicate` / `fak_syscall` or the memory apply gate.
- [Core soft locks: coherence locks for fast-moving agent code (2026-06-30)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-CORE-SOFT-LOCKS-2026-06-30.md) ŌĆö concept note for a DOS-shaped lock doctrine: hard-lock self-grading machinery, soft-lock authority/coherence changes with explicit witnesses, and keep ordinary leaf work fast.
- [Virtual-cache concept refresh: O(1) ŌåÆ vCaches, live vs gated, honest attribution (2026-06-30)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VCACHE-CONCEPT-REFRESH-2026-06-30.md) ŌĆö re-grounds every fak caching concept against the tree: the **five** distinct saving mechanisms and their owners (provider prompt-cache vs fak's compaction-shed / KV-reuse / vDSO / cache_control-preservation), which fire by default and which are **built-and-gated-OFF** (M2ŌĆōM5), and the one accounting gap ŌĆö the default `saved=` headline is **100% the provider's** prompt-cache while fak's own slice is measured but never folded in. Names the legacy assumptions to remove. Companion to the top-50 plan.
- [Top-50: caching useful-by-default and honestly attributed (2026-06-30)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VCACHE-DEFAULT-ON-TOP50-2026-06-30.md) ŌĆö 50 ranked, default-on, witnessed items to (a) decompose every saving by mechanism + owner so the provider's cache stops masquerading as fak's win, (b) make the vCache agentic loop (M2ŌĆōM5) fire by default, and (c) push the vBlock/anchor abstraction into pure-fak, API, and sglang/vllm/llama paths. Each item: what ┬Ę why ┬Ę where (file) ┬Ę default-on lever ┬Ę witness.
- [vCache gate-enablement tickets (2026-06-30)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VCACHE-GATES-ON-TICKETS-2026-06-30.md) ŌĆö the GitHub issues filed from the top-50 plan: keystone epic [#1490](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1490) + children for attribution (#1491), gate enablement (M5 #1492, M2 #1493, M1 #1497), cross-path integration (#1498), and the QA-honesty (#1495) + dogfood (#1496) contract every gate must pass before it flips default-on. Consumes #1303 (Track-2 ledger) and #1407 (anchor-starved compaction).
- [Named issue views as the default selection surface (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ISSUE-VIEWS-DEFAULT-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö the GitHub saved views at `/issues/views` aren't API-readable, so `tools/issue_views.py` + `.github/issue-views.json` give the dispatch/triage family an API-readable mirror (named views derived from the real label taxonomy); `show --json` pipes into `issue_lane_router`/`issue_triage` instead of scattered `--label` queries.
- [sm80 CUDA 13 issue #973 closure witness (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SM80-CUDA-ISSUE-973-WITNESS-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö targeted datacenter GPU-class sm_80 CUDA 13.0 witness for the two #973 residuals: `TestCUDAGraphDecodeParity` and `TestCUDAQ4KBatchedMatMulApproxMatchesRef` both pass, with Q4_K batched cosine `1.00000000`; explicitly does not claim the full original `CUDA|HALDevice` umbrella, and leaves the separate f32 failures to #972.
- [Native-harness progress tracking (#1315) (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/native-harness-progress-tracking-1315.md) ŌĆö the roll-up for [epic #1315](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1315), *fak owns the native agent loop end-to-end ŌĆö the dispatch-edge program*. On the proxy path the external harness (Claude Code/codex) owns the turn loop and *consumes* tool calls outside fak; the native harness is fak *owning* dispatch (its own loop drives the turns, the kernel is the only tool path), the precondition for every "impossible on proxy" capability (vDSO tool-result materialization, before-consumption write barrier, speculative serve). Produced from an **8-surface, 90-claim survey** whose every SHIPPED/PARTIAL claim was re-checked by an adversarial pass against git + request-path reachability ŌĆö **27 downgrades** on the one question that matters: *is there a live, non-test caller on the request path?* The honest one-liner: **the engine is owned, the loop is not (yet).** SHIPPED + live on the serve path: the native `/v1/messages` front door, in-kernel generation (`--gguf`), in-kernel tool-call render+lift+adjudication, fail-closed on malformed calls, vDSO read-serve, per-request session admission, the context planner, KV planned-elision, inbound quarantine, the `fak guard` proxy gate, the syscall boundary + A/B comparator. PARTIAL (built + tested, **zero live caller**): `RunArm` itself (only the `fak agent` benchmark + bench/ablate callers; the served path is single-shot `s.planner.Complete`), `WithSessionTable`/`gateTurn`, `drainSteer`, `WithRouteManifest`, `ApplyPace`, `WaitResume` warm-KV splice, in-loop streaming, the AgentDojo steward sweep, the multi-session scheduler, the intent conduit (#805), the loop-lifecycle ledger. NOT_YET (named gaps, keystones first): the **suspend-and-resume turn primitive** (`gateTurn` *terminates*, it does not suspend ŌĆö the one net-new mechanism the canonical note names) and the **before-consumption write barrier** (SEAM-4 `abi/speculate.go` is committed `d859ec21` but `Speculator.Predict` has zero production callers), plus the consistency-level field on `ToolCall`, the native TUI/REPL (`fak chat`), and the system-prompt-MMU spine's first live caller. 8 ordered children #1316ŌĆō#1323 (keystones #1316 a live `RunArm` caller, #1318 the suspend/resume primitive). Forward framing in [the vToolcall fork note](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VTOOLCALL-FORK-AND-BESTEFFORT-2026-06-25.md); close children per [the epic close-out method](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/EPIC-CLOSEOUT-METHOD-2026-06-29.md) (a CLOSED box is a self-report, not a witness).
- [Epic close-out method ŌĆö properly closing (or retiring) an epic (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/EPIC-CLOSEOUT-METHOD-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö the backlog is epic-heavy (57/171 open are epics); this is the repeatable method for closing one **honestly**. Guards the two failure modes ŌĆö *false-close* (a child's `CLOSED` state / `- [x]` box is a self-report, not a witness; the syspromptmmu #1258 children were docs-closed while the code stayed UNBUILT) and *stale-open* (shipped epics that nobody re-checked). Three routes: **A COMPLETED** ŌĆö every child closed AND the deliverable witnessed in the tracked tree (artifact on disk + claimed SHAs real + `dos commit-audit` OK/diff-witnessed + residual carved), closed with the #1147 house-style evidence comment (worked example: **#1116** closed 2026-06-29); **B NOT_PLANNED** ŌĆö superseded/deduped, point to the survivor and fold framing there (example #1226ŌåÆ#1217; candidate clusters: serving #50/#637/#931, agent-OS #748/#749/#750/#1178/#1193); **C STANDING** ŌĆö a "keep-the-scorecard-alive" perpetual loop (#710/#801/#1050/#1278) is retired from the deliverable count (split finite children out, drive the recurring part from a `/loop`, mark standing), never closed as done. Includes the read-only bulk close-state audit recipe and the anti-gaming laws.
- [The native-code doctrine (#1124) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/NATIVE-CODE-DOCTRINE-1124-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö fak's written rule for dropping below portable Go: Go-assembly (`.s`) is welcome on the model/compute path and is **not** cgo; `cgo`/`import "C"` is justified only for GPU device code behind an opt-in build tag; neither is ever allowed on the request/adjudication path. Plus the verified backlog of hot scalar kernels worth vectorizing (C1ŌĆōC6) and the four places native code must never go.
- [Committing shared-clone WIP is an integration pass, not a freeze (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SHARED-CLONE-INTEGRATION-PASS-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö runbook for an agent told to commit/clean up work in progress on the shared fak clone: land complete, self-contained, building units by explicit path; park the rest; gate the push with an archive-HEAD build ŌĆö instead of mislabeling collective fleet WIP as untouchable peer work.
- [fak issue-backlog dispatch loop ŌĆö handoff (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/DISPATCH-LOOP-HANDOFF-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö handoff from a session that shipped 26 issues via headless lane-disjoint workers: the paste-ready goal prompt, that session's laws, and a live backlog probe (the adversarial red-team pass was cut by a session limit, so the tooling sketches are proposals, not vetted specs).
- [GLM-5.2 multi-GPU: expert-parallel sharding + the resident-fit benchmark (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/GLM52-EXPERT-PARALLEL-MULTIGPU-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö the **expert-parallel (EP) MoE FFN decomposition** for GLM-5.2 is now host-proven on trunk (`internal/model/expert_parallel.go`, `f713391c`): partition the experts across ranks (`ExpertParallelPlan`, a named `TPPlan` over `[0,NumExperts)`), each rank computes only the picks it owns, one `AllReduceSum` of the per-rank `[H]` delta ŌĆö the cheap MoE-native multi-GPU decomposition `forwardTPSupported()` fails closed on, and the one the overnight cpu-offload data points to (move the expert GEMM off the host onto idle GPUs). The gate triad runs with **no multi-GPU hardware** (`LocalCollective`): `EP(ranks=1)`==routed monolith **max\|╬ö\|=0**, collective==rank-order ref **max\|╬ö\|=0** (ranks 1/2/4/8), `EP(ranks=N)` cosine 1.0 (~3.7e-9 reassociation drift), + load-imbalance + fail-closed + a GLM shared-expert wrapper on a real `glm_moe_dsa` fixture. Makes the first **benchmark** answerable: the GPU server's **8├Ś80GB = 640 GiB** VRAM (WITNESSED) fits GLM-5.2 UD-Q4_K_M (**433.82 GiB**, ~54 GiB/GPU even shard + replicated dense + KV Ōēł 55ŌĆō60 GiB/GPU < 80, ~20 GiB headroom, COMPUTED) ŌĆö so **resident EP is feasible by capacity**, the lever past the measured 0.2324 tok/s cpu-offload wall (vs 0.89 tok/s llama.cpp CPU). Honest scope: the primitive is landed but **not yet** wired into the live forward, and a live resident-EP tok/s witness is gated on three rungs ŌĆö a real cross-DEVICE NCCL/RCCL collective (CUDA still `cudaSetDevice(0)`, `Caps.Collective=false`), EP live-wiring, and a multi-GPU binary on the box (Go absent). The cpu-offload wall stands as the baseline until then.
- [Blackwell-class hardware readiness map (GB200/GB300 NVL72) (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/BLACKWELL-NVL72-READINESS-MAP-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö a grounded plan for running fak better on the latest NVIDIA datacenter hardware. Thesis: fak's scale-out architecture is already **NVL72-shaped** ŌĆö a coherent KV-**pool** plane (`internal/cachemeta/pool.go` `FabricShareable()`), a hardware-aware residency **ladder** (`hardware.go`, HBMŌåÆDRAMŌåÆNUMA-farŌåÆCXLŌåÆDisk), a device-**mesh** collective seam (`compute.go` `CollectiveBackend`), and a bit-exact middle-span **evict** that survives paging (`paged_evict.go`, `#33` GO) ŌĆö but **every one is a stub today** (a cost model, a CPU-ref collective, `cudaSetDevice(0)`, no FP4 enum). A GB300 NVL72 (one coherent **20.7 TB** HBM3e NVLink domain across 72 GPUs + 36 Grace CPUs, **130 TB/s** in-domain NVLink, **NVFP4** at 0.5 B/param, ~900 GB/s Grace coherent memory) is the physical substrate those planes were written against. Maps each Blackwell lever onto the exact seam it lights up (file-anchored, with `[SHIPPED]`/`[SEAM-ONLY]`/`[GAP]` state), names the four contexts where fak's *specific* levers compound (fleet prefix reuse across a coherent domain; demote-not-evict against a 900 GB/s tier; KV disaggregation at 1.8 TB/s NVLink ~18ŌĆō36├Ś the planned TCP path; the frontier class in-reach as FP4 weights stop dominating HBM and KV governance becomes the scarce resource), resolves the **one load-bearing tension** (NVFP4 vs the bit-exact-evict moat ŌåÆ tier precision per *plane*: FP4 the weights + V + read-K, keep an f32 `Kraw` shadow for governably-evictable spans, the FP4 analogue of the `#1047` Q8-KV choice), and lays a **hardware-ungated-first** rung ladder (R0 FP4+BF16 `Dtype` + cpu-ref oracle, R1 FP4-KV tier the moat survives, R2 model the NVL72 in the cost planes, R3 KVŌåÆbytes serializer ŌĆö all doable now; R5 NVFP4 device GEMM, R6 NCCL device collective, R7 NVLink/RDMA byte mover, R8 the governed mega-pool ŌĆö Blackwell-gated). Strategic frame: the hardware doesn't make fak a *faster server* (vLLM/SGLang still win tok/s) ŌĆö it makes fak's **governance** value mandatory, because a 20.7 TB coherent pool shared by a fleet *forces* the cross-tenant-reuse / provable-evict / placement / disaggregation questions only fak answers with attribution. Asserts **no** measured fak number on Blackwell; every figure is a published spec or a labelled MODELED cost-model projection (the `#44` parity-must-be-measured rule still binds).
- [Cache savings in one session: what fak did and didn't (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SESSION-CACHE-SAVINGS-ABLATION-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö ablates a `fak guard` session (`c6723f7c`, 122 turns) three ways ŌĆö **no cache (~29.65M projected)**, **normal Anthropic cache (~4.15M, ŌłÆ86%)**, **fak guard (~4.15M, identical)** ŌĆö to make the attribution honest: the `~25.5M input-token-equiv` headline is the **provider's** prompt cache, which fak **prices** (`cache_pricing.go`, OBSERVED not WITNESSED) but cannot move, because passthrough forwards `req.Raw` verbatim and the `cache_control` prefix stays byte-identical. fak's *own* contribution is a sliver of uncached tool-def tokens (tool-floor prune, ~0.1% of headline) and ŌĆö the real lever ŌĆö **1,104 bad tool calls stopped before a wasted round-trip** (1,101 repaired, 3 denied); compaction stayed idle. Also answers **"does that mean fak's features aren't on?"** with a HEAD-verified enablement audit ŌĆö all six token-savers are default-on (`token_defaults.go`-locked) incl. `ctxview`/`elide` (flipped on 06-28/06-26; #927 closed the #555 passthrough gap), but the cache-breakpoint-near-the-end structure leaves fak only a small uncached tail to act on, by design (a referee), not a misconfig. Two-panel [ablation SVG](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/session-cache-ablation-2026-06-29.svg) + [enablement-status SVG](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/session-feature-enablement-2026-06-29.svg).
- [Audit: recent fak logs ŌĆö effectiveness + context fidelity (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/AUDIT-recent-fak-logs-effectiveness-fidelity-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö the **operational** companion to the [harness-cache-coherence design audit](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/HARNESS-CACHE-COHERENCE-AUDIT-2026-06-28.md), driven by the *recent* logs on the clone (`.fak/`). Answers the two questions directly. **(1) How effective?** From today's `recent-feature-dogfood` log: vCache `7.13├Ś` telemetry readiness (one workload, OBSERVED), callavoid `2.46├Ś` turn-amplification, cache-value `63%` realized reuse ŌĆö but the load-bearing finding is the **honest ceiling**: `internal/cachevalueledger/ledger.go:111-119` hard-codes the only publishable framing as `~1.0├Ś single-session (vs-naive 1/(1-reuse) excluded per #1066)`, and the `--floor 0.751` gate *correctly abstained* because `MinGateTurns=8` and only 5 turns were present. The dogfood's overall `ACTION` verdict comes from two **honest** failures, neither a context fault: a probe that refuses to call a *planned* 2├Ś a *telemetry-witnessed* 2├Ś (`recent_feature_dogfood.py:348-355` requires `active_source=="telemetry"`), and a transient assembler build error (`internal/model/quant_amd64_kquant.s` `CMPQ $0,AX` ŌĆö green at HEAD when re-checked, so a stale-log artifact). **(2) How do we know it isn't butchering context?** Five tested invariants ŌĆö verbatim-prefix byte-splice (`anthropic_compact.go`/`anthropic_elide.go`, a turn only counts `fired` if the protected prefix was byte-identical, else `prefix_mismatch`, `metrics.go:104`), bail-to-identity on any ambiguity with a closed reason vocabulary, bounded+marked+never-total elision loss (head+tail, last-4 working set untouched), the witness-gated quarantine moat (`recall.go:18-22`), and single-source WITNESSED/OBSERVED-separated attribution. Names the three **honest fidelity limits** rather than hiding them: KV prefix-elision is admittedly NOT bit-exact (`exact=false`, "a re-RoPE cannot un-see attended history", `kvmmu_elide_test.go:51-76`); the quarantine-survives-harness-summary hole is *detected* (`QuarantineAtRisk`) not fixed; and the `PreCompact` suppression **actuator is not yet wired** (`harness_coherence.go:283`, #1133 open) ŌĆö fak today *measures* the harness butchering context but does not yet *prevent* it. Verdict: effectiveness REAL-but-fenced (passes net-true-value), fidelity STRONG-by-construction + HONEST-about-the-gaps. The mechanism even acted **live on the auditing session** (a `ctxview`-elided turn + a credential-shaped string paged out, both announced with a stub). Lists three checkable `not yet` follow-ons.
- [Context-safety doctrine (#1218, C1 of epic #1217) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/context-safety-visuals-tracking-1217.md) ŌĆö the C1 design note for the context-safety epic [#1217](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1217): states the **property** (context safety = value-preservation, the **dual** of the security floor ŌĆö the security floor proves a *bad* call can't get *in*, this floor proves a *good* call's value doesn't silently get *lost* when fak sheds context, and shows it as a cross-checkable picture, not a number you must trust), grounded in why a single number can't carry it (the measured `ctxplan/signalnoise` cache-hit denominator artifact: 0.88ŌåÆ0.99 mechanically with length). Names the **context-event source of truth** ŌĆö the hash-chained journal `internal/journal/journal.go:59-90` (`CAP_FAULT | CAP_EVICT | CAP_VERSION_BIND` rows, `Seq`/`PrevHash`/`Hash`/`Witness`, replayable via `Verify`/`VerifyRows`) plus the two secondary sources (`engine/capacity_adapter.go` `CacheEventRecorder`; `cachemeta/kvtransfer.go` `KVTransfer` ok/missed/fault). Decomposes "real retained value" into **four guarantees**, each bound to a real cited witness seam: **G1** bit-identity `max\|╬ö\|=0` evict-correctness (`kvcache.go:44-105`; `paged_evict_test.go:84`, `longrope_test.go:179`), **G2** saved-vs-realized reuse-bit + correctness (WITNESSED `fak_gateway_kv_prefix_reused_tokens_total` `cachewitness.go:155` reconciled against `prefix_coherence.go:51`), **G3** S/N + Faults paired (`signalnoise.go` `Ratio()`/`FaultRatio()`/`Grade()` `starving`), **G4** cell-integrity (`recall.PageSyndrome` five-axis digest re-derivation). States the **self-checking-visual doctrine** (A paired-axis / B conservation roll-up / C provenance lane / D re-derivation, generalizing the `RestoreIndex(ix.Image()) == ix` rebuild-and-prove-equal pattern to the rendered visual) and the **honesty fences** (value-preservation kept distinct from the security floor AND the #1147 self-tax plane; WITNESSED/OBSERVED/MODELED never summed; an un-witnessed guarantee is a `not yet` not a result; design-only). Names the honest `not yet` gaps ŌĆö the re-derivation witness (C9 #1227), the cell-integrity visual surface, the DOS refusal tokens (C13 #1231) ŌĆö rather than rendering them green. Research/design only; sibling of the C2 failure-mode catalog [#1219](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1219).
- [Context-safety failure-mode catalog (#1219, C2 of epic #1217) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/context-safety-failure-mode-catalog-1217.md) ŌĆö the C2 child of the context-safety epic [#1217](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1217): every failure the value-preservation plane must surface, each mapped to its **witness** (the journal `Kind` / typed verdict / metric / test that catches it), the visual **primitive** that renders it, and the **cross-check** (paired-axis / conservation roll-up / provenance lane / re-derivation) that goes red on it. Six grounded rows ŌĆö GDN evict panic (#1143, `RecurrentEvictUnsupportedError` + `fak_bgloop_up`-vs-decode-rate liveness light), evict-on-recurrent refused (the `refused` conservation segment), saved-but-never-reused (the WITNESSED `fak_gateway_kv_prefix_reused_tokens_total` gap chart, never blended with OBSERVED provider `cache_read`), S/N-up-but-faults-up (the `ctxplan/signalnoise` `Ratio()`+`FaultRatio()` `starving` grade), cell rot (#782 `recall.PageSyndrome`'s five-axis digest/quarantine/durability/witness/trust-epoch re-derivation), tier page-down missed (`cachemeta.KVTransfer` ok/missed/fault, ties #985) ŌĆö each cited to a real `file:line`. Research/design only; sibling of the C1 doctrine note [#1218](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1218).
- [Context-safety saved-vs-realized gap (#1221, C4 of epic #1217) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/context-safety-saved-vs-realized-gap-1217.md) ŌĆö the C4 value-decomposition spec (MVP slice) for the context-safety epic [#1217](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1217): the one value-gap that matters ŌĆö **claimed-saved tokens at page-out time vs later-realized reuse** ŌĆö built from two WITNESSED taps, **D1** (claimed-saved: journal `CAP_EVICT` row + `internal/engine/cacheevents.go:304-317` `CacheEventRecorder`) and **D2** (realized reuse: `internal/cachewitness` `KVPrefixWitness.ReuseRatio()` `cachewitness.go:155` over `fak_gateway_kv_prefix_reused_tokens_total` `metrics.go:1531`, fed by `cacheobs.Default.Snapshot()`). Specs the `SavedRealizedGap` datum (`Gap = ClaimedSaved - RealizedReused`, the error bar; a positive gap is an over-claim fak never realized), the cross-checks it carries (A paired-axis saved-with-realized + D re-derive both from tamper-evident sources, the C9 checker), and the load-bearing **provenance fence**: the gap is **never blended with the OBSERVED provider `cache_read`** ŌĆö grounded in the measured cache-hit denominator artifact (`cache_read` climbs to ~1.0 mechanically with length, so a gap built on it would close to zero precisely when the window is most bloated). Binds guarantee G2 of the C1 doctrine (#1218); renders failure F3 of the C2 catalog (#1219); reconciled against the C14 coherence witness (#1232); re-derived by C9 (#1227). Research/design only.

Lifts four shipped precedents (`image.go`, journal `Verify`, `check_memory.py`, `dispatch_status.py` strict diff-witnessed, the `metrics.go` `proven` vCache gate). CI-gate sibling is C10 (#1228). - [The agent programming grammar ŌĆö lifting fak's invariants into a domain-free substrate (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-AGENT-PROGRAMMING-GRAMMAR-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö the "express + generalize" half of the innovations survey: fak's contribution is one invariant (*a decision no participant can move by narrating a number*) carried at every scale, not any single primitive (0/29 novel). Extracts that invariant as a reusable **grammar** ŌĆö nouns (lane, lease, reason token, witness, verdict, claim, ladder rung, scope) and verbs (arbitrate, verify, audit, review, refuse, recall, resolve, status) ŌĆö names the ~10 fak concepts already lifted into the domain-free DOS form, the two structural shapes a correct primitive obeys (the verification ladder; the security==optimization two lenses), and scopes the **under-expressed** concepts (`G1ŌĆōG9`: readiness ladder, verification-ladder verb, durability-class promotion, materialized-view context contract, prediction-vs-reality calibration, one-sided-screen polarity, taint-check, per-aspect routing, net-true-value claim-check) as the next grammar verbs. The companion of the durable [innovations index](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/INNOVATIONS-INDEX.md); actioned by the grammar epic.
- [The feature-maturity lifecycle ladder ŌĆö completeness is not maturity, and every gap is the next work item (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-MATURITY-LADDER-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö the **shipped** subsystem for the long-horizon "agentic culture" problem: a v1 prototype can be legitimately *complete* yet not tested, not dogfooded, not benchmarked, not the default ŌĆö and the operator should SEE where each capability sits and what would mature it next. [`internal/maturity`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/tree/main/internal/maturity) places every declared fak capability (one per `internal/<leaf>` lane in [`dos.toml`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/dos.toml) `[lanes.trees]`) on a closed lifecycle ladder ŌĆö `proposed ŌåÆ prototyped ŌåÆ tested ŌåÆ dogfooded ŌåÆ default` (with a `benchmarked` badge) ŌĆö each rung gated by evidence the author did not write (code on disk, a `*_test.go`, an edge in the running binary's transitive import graph, a documented verb), so a rung moves only by changing the real tree. **Immaturity is never a defect** (a complete v1 honestly sitting at `prototyped` is expected); the one thing it refuses is a **ladder-skip** ŌĆö a capability fak relies on yet leaves untested (`maturity_debt`, the maturity sibling of the readiness ladder's `READINESS_OVERCLAIM`). For each capability the first unmet rung is rendered as a concrete **next work item**, and `fak maturity next` is the ranked backlog the issue-dispatch loop pulls from ŌĆö the mechanization of "each agent has the desire to create the next ticket." The shipped fak-tree binding of grammar [G1 / the readiness ladder](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-AGENT-PROGRAMMING-GRAMMAR-2026-06-28.md); first dogfood run over fak's own 109 lanes: index 78/100, 0 ladder-skips, 18 tested-but-not-dogfooded.
- [Complain by default ŌĆö the agentic-dev friction channels and when to use each (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-AGENT-FRICTION-COMPLAINT-CHANNELS-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö a playbook for the `/goal` "improve the default ability for agents to complain about annoying agentic-dev things": friction that no agent records stays trapped in per-agent private memory and is re-discovered by the next agent, so the default response to a papercut should be to register it through a structured, **deduplicating** channel that is part of the repo. Names a closed, evidence-grounded **friction taxonomy** (`shared-tree-clobber`, `red-trunk`, `stamp-refusal`, `push-blocked`, `saturated-box`, `tool-timeout`, `lane-collision`, `guard-false-positive`, `confusing-refusal`, `phantom-artifact`) and routes each to the channel that fits *who acts and how fast* ŌĆö operator/status surfacing via `fak blockers post|feed` (shipped), the capability-floor **appeal** that must carry the witnessed journal verdict (`internal/guardcomplaint`; the `fak complain` dispatch is `not yet` wired ŌĆö mid-rework on the shared tree), the DOS closed **refusal vocabulary** (`dos_refuse_reasons`/`dos_check_reason`) to decline green with a verifiable reason, and the `internal/dogfoodissues` dedup/escalate fold for recurring process friction. Four rules for complaining as signal not noise (structured+deduped, carry a witness not a feeling, name the recovery you wanted, route by who acts). Companion of the [agent programming grammar](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-AGENT-PROGRAMMING-GRAMMAR-2026-06-28.md); the `not yet` half (wire the verb + a `workflow`/`dev` domain) is the next checkable step.
- [Affordable-hardware agent fleets ŌĆö the doable tier, and what shows it (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/AFFORDABLE-HARDWARE-AGENT-FLEETS-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö a strategy/framing + showable-demo map for running **multiple** coding agents **safely** on hardware a small team can afford (one big-RAM CPU box, one or a few older datacenter GPU/Ampere GPUs, a gaming GPU, or a Mac), including overnight/offline. Thesis: the agent fleet is the workload where affordable hardware wins, because it is latency-tolerant (batch it overnight), prefix-heavy (pay the setup once), and unattended (so trust, not tok/s, binds). Separates the **witnessed-today** tier (sub-frontier Ōēż27B ŌĆö the 27B @64ŌĆō128-concurrency curve, the 4070 862-agg-tok/s batch, the Mac 4.1├Ś fleet stack, the 1,024-agent CPU `fanrun`, the GPU-free `fleet-reuse-demo`) from the **honest not-yet** (frontier 753B GLM-5.2 on CPU-offload at sub-1 tok/s ŌĆö the #971 host-expert wall; TPU = no backend), and ties each tier to the specific fak lever (RadixAttention prefix reuse, continuous batching, resident-Q4_K, capacity preflight) and safety rail (default-deny capabilities, `dos_arbitrate` disjoint lanes, commit-audit, quarantine) that makes it usable. Every measured number cited to its authority row; every projection labeled.

- [Two cache stacks, one wire ŌĆö fak's cache concepts vs the agent harness's, and do we disable Claude Code auto-compaction? (#1131) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/HARNESS-CACHE-COHERENCE-AUDIT-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö audit of the coherence between fak's cache/context machinery (cache-preserving compaction #745, budget reset / cut-vs-reset #774, prompt-MMU #751, ctxmmu quarantine, paged-KV evict, cachemeta prefix-stability/coherence) and the agent harness's OWN machinery (Claude Code auto-compaction, `cache_control` prompt-cache, ~5-min TTL). Names the interference matrix: the two managers stack blind to each other ŌĆö fak is cache-**preserving**, the harness's auto-compaction is cache-**destroying** (it rewrites the `cache_control` prefix, bursting the cache fak just preserved) and can defeat a quarantine by folding a sealed span into its summary. Answers the explicit question with evidence: Claude Code auto-compaction **cannot be reliably disabled via settings/env** (the toggle is silently ignored; threshold clamped ~83%; anthropics/claude-code #38483/#42817/#42149) ŌĆö the one dependable lever is a **`PreCompact` hook (exit 2)**, so the answer is a conditional protocol (**block** while fak copes, **allow** when fak's compaction bails, **reset** past TTL), not a flag flip. Lands the spine [`internal/compactcohere`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/internal/compactcohere/compactcohere.go): a pure tier-1 sensor that attributes a per-turn prefix event (`stable`/`fak_cut`/`fak_world_break`/`harness_rewrite`/`cold_ttl`) + a standing block/allow PreCompact posture, reusing `cachemeta` divergence rather than duplicating it. Actuators (gateway wiring, the guard hook, the quarantine-survival witness) are the epic.

- [Evict-on-paged KV ŌĆö the bit-exact middle-span Evict survives a block-paged layout (#33) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/EVICT-ON-PAGED-KV-DESIGN-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö design + prototype proof for [#33](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/33) (the gate before the native paged allocator [#34](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/34) and the L3 governance referee [#27](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/27)). Verdict **GO**: fak's kernel-owned, bit-exact middle-span `KVCache.Evict` carries forward onto a block-paged layout because the single-rotation re-RoPE of a shifted survivor depends only on its pre-RoPE `Kraw` and its **new logical position**, not on physical contiguity. Two requirements proven on real float32 KV with no GPU: the paged blocks must carry a per-token `Kraw` plane (the shipped #277 allocator stored only K/V ŌĆö `NewPagedKVPoolWithRaw` adds it), and a survivor re-RoPE must copy-on-write-split any shared block before mutating K. A mid-span evict on a deliberately non-contiguous page table is **byte-for-byte identical** to the contiguous `Evict` (cache **and** next-token logits, `max|╬ö|=0` on amd64), and a COW-forked sibling is left untouched (`internal/model/paged_evict.go`, `internal/model/paged_evict_test.go`). Names the honest cost (an intra-block compaction on middle-span evict, which vLLM/SGLang avoid only because they cannot evict a middle span at all), the FP8/INT8-KV decision (keep `Kraw` f32 per [#1047](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/1047); defer quantized-K-with-exact-span), and scopes the GLM-DSA path out with reason (its `glm.rerotateSurvivor` follows the identical new-logical-index re-derivation; carrying the DSA state onto blocks is #34's job).
- [`L3RegionBackend` ŌĆö child B of epic #79, designed behind the frozen `Ref`/`Resolver`/`RegionBackend` seam (#55) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/L3REGIONBACKEND-CHILD-B-DESIGN-55-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö the `design(` artifact for [#55](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/55) (Option B): the interface, spanŌåÆpage-key contract, resolve/page-in/page-out/evict lifecycle, G2 middle-eviction fold, and G3 deletion-certificate tier-projection that unlock **G2** (evict a poisoned span from the *middle* of pages shared across sequences) and native **G3** (attest the *L3 pages* were destroyed, not just the local copy) ŌĆö neither reachable from a control-path-only sidecar. Specifies `L3RegionBackend` as a **backend swap** under the frozen `abi.RegionBackend` seam (`internal/abi/registry.go:609`) with **no ABI edit**, each method named against the external store's `mget`/`mset`/evict, the per-page `Ref.Digest` verify stated as a **fail-closed** page-in admission gate (G1), `evict(from,n)` mapped through fak-owned `KVCache.Evict` re-rotation (bit-exact `max|╬ö|=0` survivors) to an L3 page-key delete + re-key, and `deletioncert.Mint` folded to attest the L3 page-key set offline-verifiably. Honest spine: the in-kernel engine (v0.2) OR a co-residence handshake is a **HARD [GAP] ~120h-class precondition**, the design stays on the **control path** (┬¦6.5: opt-in admission verify, not an inline per-RDMA-read byte-scan; ~1ŌĆō5 ┬Ąs L3 read budget named), the staged plan gates run on a **mock/local** L3 (real-store wire optional/follow-on), it composes with [#493](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/493) (network `StageTransport`) for the multi-node case, and the whole backend is **gated behind Option A** (the referee sidecar) proving the seam first. The design row [`L3-DISAGGREGATED-CACHE-REIMAGINED`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/L3-DISAGGREGATED-CACHE-REIMAGINED.md) (epic [#79](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/79)) labelled "child B".

- [The readiness / durability verdict ladder as a domain-free DOS primitive (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/CONCEPT-DOS-READINESS-VERDICT-LADDER-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö concept capture for [#582](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/582): the mechanism behind the product scorecard ([`tools/product_scorecard.py`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/tools/product_scorecard.py) / [PRODUCT-STATUS](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/PRODUCT-STATUS.md)) is not fak-specific ŌĆö it is a generic DOS primitive. A **closed maturity ladder** (`durable-product ŌåÆ ŌĆ” ŌåÆ concept-only`) where every rung's promotion is gated by **evidence the claimant did not author** (the CLAIMS tag a concept carries, whether its first command resolves to a real `cmd/` dir + documented verb, whether its witness/entry docs exist on disk), plus a **surface-ceiling gate** that stops a benchmark from posing as a durable product and a stub from posing as shipped ŌĆö so a verdict can only move by changing the real tree, never the data. The note (1) extracts the surface-gate + evidence-binding core into a domain-free five-part form (closed rung vocabulary, surfaceŌåÆceiling cap, capability set, evidence predicates over ground truth, evidence-bound adjudication with overclaim-refusal), (2) sketches a `dos`-grounded variant where the top rung is closed by a `dos` **witness** (`dos_verify` reads git ancestry, `dos_commit_audit` separates a `diff-witnessed` claim from a forgeable `subject-only` one) and an overclaim is refused with a structured reason from a closed vocabulary (cf. `dos_refuse_reasons`, proposed token `READINESS_OVERCLAIM`), and (3) decides where it lives: **keep the tree-cross-check shell a fak tool, lift the closed-ladder + ceiling-gate + evidence-bound-adjudication + refusal core into the DOS concept vocabulary** ŌĆö building a `dos readiness` verb is a dos-kernel follow-up named as the next step, not committed here (the issue scoped this to concept capture, no code).
- [One machine: the session lifecycle IS serving admission (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SESSION-LIFECYCLE-IS-SERVING-ADMISSION-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö explores the parallel between session start/resume and dynamic fused serving of random-length, flexible-turn sessions. The thesis: the `internal/session` run-state machine (start/throttle/pause/drain/stop + cold `Restore` + `Recontinue`) and the continuous-batch serving operation (admit/preempt/evict/resume a sequence) are **one state machine at two altitudes**, with the agent **turn boundary** as the single admission/preemption/resume quantum. fak already implies this but spells the machine twice (`session.RunState` vs `loopmgr.LoopState`, two enums sharing `paused/draining/stopped`) and only advisory-couples the halves (`TurnIntent` is consumed by prewarm/discard admission but gates no live served batch). Maps the verified correspondence (session verb Ōåö serving verb Ōåö status Ōåö grounding symbol), separates the buildable seams (shared vocabulary, one epoch lineage, `SlotEvent`ŌåÆKV reclaim, warm-swap on resume) from the analogy-only rows (cross-host live migration; `KVIncluded=false` by design), positions against prior art ([#805](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/805)/[#748](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/748)/[#637](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/637)/[#809](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/809)/[#844](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/844)), and proposes the unification epic [#912](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/912) (children [#913](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/913)ŌĆō[#916](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/916)). Decomposition adversarially verified by a multi-agent workflow; every cited file:symbol and issue state confirmed against the tree.
- [Is witnessed span-attention a valid reward? Verdict, aggregation, and failure modes (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-witnessed-span-attention-validity-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö prior-art survey 1/5 for the reward-over-spans epic [#860](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/860): the attention-as-explanation debate resolved into a usable rule for [#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861). Verdict: witnessed span-attention is a cheap, relative, span-level proposal signal, not a standalone causal explanation or accepted reward until it correlates with exact-eviction leave-one-out. The note maps Jain/Wallace, Wiegreffe/Pinter, Serrano/Smith, and Abnar/Zuidema to fak's actual post-softmax observer and span ledger, then pins the aggregation rule: consumer rows only, span-level mass, all observed heads/layers summed for direct cache-read accounting, position/length normalization, and top/bottom LOO validation before any live planner or RSI promotion. Satisfies [#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861).
- [Span-attention confound normalization: sinks, position, recency, and length (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-span-attention-confound-normalization-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö prior-art survey 2/5 for the reward-over-spans epic [#860](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/860): the normalization contract for [#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862). Raw attention is demoted to telemetry unless `expected_attention_mass` subtracts sink/preamble positions, massive-activation/outlier token effects, span length, relative-position/U-shaped long-context bias, and distance-to-query/recency defaults. The note defines the null-model rungs (uniform-visible fallback, position-length baseline, distance/age baseline, sink mask, per-head/layer calibration) and maps them to `SpanRewardSegment.ExpectedAttentionMass`, `NormalizedReward`, and `ConfoundNormalizationImproved`, preserving the rule that exact-eviction LOO decides whether the residual attention actually mattered. Satisfies [#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862).

- [Mac Qwen3.6-27B Metal Q4_K perf diagnosis: decode 1.2 / prefill 0.6 tok/s, the per-call command-buffer wall (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/MAC-QWEN36-27B-Q4K-METAL-PERF-DIAGNOSIS-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö a clean on-device measurement (M3 Pro, `-tags fakmetal`, `FAK_Q4K=1 FAK_METAL=1`, llama-server stopped) of fak's resident-Q4_K Metal path for the big Mac model, toward the 3├Ś decode goal.
