Re-prioritizes epic [#59](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/59): the kernel children (#515 q4_k GEMM / #516 resident weights / #517 decode GEMV / #71 hybrid prefill) have effectively **landed**, so the binding levers are now [#67](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/67) (one MTLCommandBuffer per forward ŌĆö the GPU-resident decode forward, decode twin of the shipped `mg_prefill`) and a kernel-efficiency pass. Witness: `experiments/benchmark/runs/by-machine/node-macos-a/20260626T055239Z-q4k-metal-decode-27b/score.json`.
- [Mac Qwen2.5-7B Q8 GPU-resident decode forward: at performant parity with llama.cpp-Metal (0.99├Ś) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/MAC-QWEN25-7B-Q8-RESIDENT-DECODE-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö the [#67](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/67) one-command-buffer GPU-resident **dense Q8 decode forward** (the decode twin of the shipped `mg_prefill`), built, optimized and measured on the M3 Pro. **Correct** ŌĆö logit cosine **0.999991** + greedy token-parity vs the CPU Q8 decode (`TestMetalDecodeResidentMatchesCPU`), and the real Qwen2.5-1.5B Q8 emits the same text as CPU (`"...France is" -> "Paris"`); engages on Qwen2.5-7B Q8 with attention bias. **Six shipped optimizations took clean 7B Q8 decode 10.0 -> 17.6 tok/s (0.56├Ś -> 0.988├Ś of llama.cpp-Metal 17.82) ŌĆö AT PARITY** (within ~1.2%, inside run-to-run variance): a **parallel RMSNorm** (the v0 ran the H-wide norm on ONE GPU thread ├Ś56/token ŌĆö a matmul-only split found it; +0.24├Ś), the **LM head + final norm on the GPU** (+0.08├Ś), **f16 block scales** + fused bias (GGUF Q8_0 std, ~6% fewer bytes; +0.04├Ś), **persistent on-GPU KV** (seed once, append in place; +0.025├Ś), and **key-parallel flash-decode attention** (8 simdgroups/head split the keys + flash-combine ŌĆö a skip-attention split isolated the serial O(ctx) attention as the last residual; +0.04├Ś). fak's projection GEMVs already run at **~154 GB/s, ABOVE llama's ~143**, and the matmul+head floor (~55.5 ms) is below llama's full decode (~56.1 ms). Shipped `5a11a31`..`d6188182`. Continues [MAC-QWEN36-27B-Q4K-METAL-PERF-DIAGNOSIS](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/MAC-QWEN36-27B-Q4K-METAL-PERF-DIAGNOSIS-2026-06-26.md).
- [idea-scout triage: out-of-band injection-defense taxonomy + adaptive evaluation (#909) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-out-of-band-injection-defense-taxonomy-triage-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26479 (Narisetty/Kore/Kattamanchi/Kumarapu, "Adaptive Evaluation of Out-of-Band Defenses Against Prompt Injection in LLM Agents"): the **survey that names fak's own family** ŌĆö defend a tool-using agent against indirect prompt injection NOT by training the model to refuse but by enforcing security *outside* the model with a deterministic policy that mediates its actions (CaMeL/FIDES/Progent/RTBAS/FORGE), organized as classical **reference monitoring + Biba integrity + least privilege**. Verdict: **prior art to cite** ŌĆö fak IS an out-of-band defense (default-deny capability floor = reference monitor + least privilege, IFC `Ref.Taint` sink-gating = Biba integrity, result-admit quarantine = containment that holds when the model is fooled), so the taxonomy is the academic name for the seams [`docs/industry-scorecard/security.md`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/industry-scorecard/security.md) already calls "FIDES/CaMeL-class". The paper's second contribution ŌĆö every one of these defenses is validated only on **static** benchmarks, the same methodology that made in-band defenses look strong until adaptive attacks broke twelve at >90% ŌĆö independently backs fak's deliberate non-load-bearing-detection bet (~100% evadable, by design) while **raising the bar** fak's own `internal/agentdojo` ASR battery should meet (adaptive, defense-aware, not static); the paper's own adaptive result is one small black-box data point (Progent on AgentDojo, Qwen2.5-7B/H200, mean ASR 25.8%ŌåÆ4.2%, adaptive 2.6%, white-box GCG still open), cited as a protocol to adopt for evaluation, never as evidence fak's containment is adaptively robust. No capability adopted (fak already enforces out-of-band); not a threat (a parallel defense on fak's side of the trust boundary). Resolved by `e7a6607`.
- [Compaction vs. the industry: drop-and-splice vs summarize-and-resend (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/COMPACTION-VS-INDUSTRY-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö a sourced audit of how fak's cache-prefix-preserving history compaction differs from the built-in compaction in Claude Code, the Anthropic API context-editing feature, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Aider, and LangChain. Industry mechanisms web-sourced; every fak claim adversarially verified against `internal/agent/anthropic_compact.go` and the gateway. The crux: almost everyone summarizes/clears and re-sends a **rewritten** prompt (which the providers' own docs say breaks the prompt cache); fak drops middle turns and **byte-splices** so the cached prefix stays byte-identical (`bytes.Equal` proven, 13 fail-safe identity exits). Records the four disambiguations that survived adversarial review ŌĆö the provider cache **cascade** is an unverified hypothesis not a measured fact (the audit's biggest open risk; the `/metrics` instrument exists, it needs one credentialed Anthropic session, [#745](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/745)); fak vs Anthropic context-editing is **orthogonal** (cache-preservation vs recoverable tool-result clearing), not "better"; "lossless" is **overloaded** ŌåÆ a four-axis table (cache-bytes / trust-boundary / model-context / recoverability); Anthropic-wire-shaped **by implementation, not necessity**. Adds the durable surface: a new `client-compaction` dimension in the industry scorecard (a categorical byte-splice **lead** row + an explicit **no-claim** on realized cache reuse).
- [idea-scout triage: attention-located malicious-skill detection (#771) (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-malicious-agent-skills-attention-triage-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.23416 ("Detecting Malicious Agent Skills in the Wild using Attention", Etteib/Lunghi/Bissyand├®): marketplace skills run with user privileges, and the paper's load-bearing claim is that prompt-injection defenses collapse because a skill *is* instructions (the trusted/untrusted-data boundary disappears). Its **Locate-and-Judge** detector scores skill spans by instruction-following **attention** and judges only top-K, auditing a marketplace at order-of-magnitude lower cost. Verdict: **prior art to cite** ŌĆö it independently validates *why* fak gates the **capability** a skill exercises rather than classifying its instructions (exactly the boundary the paper shows collapses) ŌĆö plus a real threat whose action step (exfiltrate=send, hijack=destructive call, persist=write) fak's default-deny capability floor already gates a layer **below** a static pre-load scanner. The attention-locator is **not adopted** (best-effort, no kernel load surface, and its attention-is-importance premise is what fak's own [#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861)/[#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862) is still auditing).
- [fak in-kernel CUDA serve on an L4: live tok/s + a concurrency crash fixed (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/L4-INKERNEL-SERVE-AND-CONCURRENCY-FIX-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the reachable-hardware companion to the [GPU server cross-engine](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/DGX2-CROSS-ENGINE-DATA-2026-06-25.md) note: with the 8-GPU server nodes reachable only through a down control-bridge, this records what could be measured on the one live GPU node (the `fak-realmodel` GCP L4). Two results. (1) The first **warm steady-state** decode rate against fak's *live* in-kernel CUDA serve: a real Qwen2.5-0.5B Q8_0 decodes at **~466 tok/s** single-stream (median of 8, range 437ŌĆō510; ~2.25 GB of 23 GB VRAM) ŌĆö an order of magnitude over the WSL-laptop Q8 numbers, confirming [GPU-QWEN-RESULTS](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/GPU-QWEN-RESULTS.md)'s thesis that the laptop gap was the WSL launch tax, not the kernel (the *cold* first decode is ~66 tok/s and must not be quoted as steady-state). (2) The serving bug live traffic exposed: a concurrent `/v1/messages` burst **took the GPU serve down** ŌĆö the single-stream CUDA backend (one `g_stream`, one cuBLAS handle, a shared device free-list) was driven by two concurrent `InKernelPlanner.Complete` goroutines whose per-token op sequences interleaved and corrupted each other's device buffers ŌåÆ sticky `cuda_kernels.cu` illegal-memory-access ŌåÆ poisoned context until restart (the sibling **CPU** serve answered correctly throughout, isolating the fault). Fixed by holding a dedicated `devMu` across the whole forward when a backend is wired (safe queuing, correct for a single-stream device; CPU path untouched), with a **proven non-vacuous** GPU-free test (3719 op-overlaps without the lock, 0 with it). Honestly scoped: a small-model device-decode serving rate, not a 753B / GPU server / stock-engine number.
- [Credit assignment over spans: PRMs, RL credit, agent-memory usefulness (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-credit-assignment-over-spans-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö prior-art survey 4/5 for the reward-over-spans epic [#860](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/860): how the RL / agent-training literature assigns credit to intermediate steps and retrieved items, and the span-credit-assignment design those patterns produce. Folds four families into one reward equation ŌĆö `reward(s,T) = success_gate(T) ┬Ę ╬Ż ╬│^(TŌłÆt) ┬Ę m_s(t)` ŌĆö with each term justified by a named pattern (**outcome gate** from PRMs / Math-Shepherd; **recency discount** from TD(╬╗) eligibility traces / GAE; **confound normalization** from attention-sink + the "attention is/is-not explanation" contest) and each mapped onto a fak seam that already ships (`ctxplan.OutcomeFromAttention` produces it, `Forecast.Learn` / `Weights.Learn` consume it into the `recall.Page.Utility` Q-slot, `rsiloop` keeps-or-reverts on witnessed signal/noise). Every term is **degenerate-to-current** (identity knobs reproduce the shipped flat cumulative attention) so [#866](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/866) can ablate each independently; carries the epic's honest spine verbatim ŌĆö attention-as-importance is **contested**, so the producer is a swap point that takes the leave-one-out signal ([#863](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/863)) at the same `Outcome` interface if validation refutes attention, and the outcome gate demands a **witnessed** verdict, never the model's self-report.
- [Shared live trunk vs. branch / worktree / VM-per-agent (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SHARED-TRUNK-VS-PER-AGENT-ISOLATION-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the positioning half of epic [#822](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/822) / [#834](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/834): the field (Cursor background agents, Devin, Sculptor, OpenHands, Dagger `container-use`, Google GitFarm) converged on **never share a tree** ŌĆö branch/worktree/VM per agent, integration gated on a human merge. That validates fak's contrarian bet (one **live trunk** + **pre-call adjudication** + **disjoint-lease admission**) while giving it **nothing to import** for the hard part, because every surveyed system *avoids* the shared-tree problem rather than solving it. Names the trade as a table (what each side buys/costs), the three named git-admin gaps with their current closure status at HEAD, and carries the epic's honesty caveats **verbatim**: `mvdan/sh` cannot resolve `$VAR`/`alias`/`eval` (fail closed); `grite` = distribution **not** atomicity + the **synthetic** "78%ŌåÆ0%"; a signature is an **identity envelope only** (the SHA already content-addresses); AgenticFlict **sizes the problem, never claims fak's benefit**.
- [Fusion, ref-counting, and GC for the agent loop (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/FUSION-REFCOUNT-GC-FOR-THE-AGENT-LOOP-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the runtime-heap lens on the loop: every turn and tool result is a **heap object**, a pinned goal / active slash-command / open task is a **GC root**, compaction is **garbage collection**. The decisive finding after mapping the tree: the collector machine already ships at the byte/KV depths (`abi.CASPinner` refcount-by-digest, `ProvisionalSink.Promote/Rollback` commit-or-collect, `ctxplan.Forecast.Pins` whose own doc names "the active goal" as the canonical pin, `cachemeta.Lifecycle` TTL+`Touch`-revive) ŌĆö what is missing is the single **edge from an intentional root to the heap objects it keeps alive**. Locates the gap exactly at `SessionPlanner.pins()` (the complete current root-set is system/first-user/last-user; the goal is absent), defines the **ref-count of a turn** (its live referents: an open goal, a later citing turn, a pending consumer), the conservative over-retain safety asymmetry (a false-free silently loses needed context; a false-retain costs a few tokens), **turn-fusion** (collapse a refcount-1 producerŌåÆconsumer intermediate, the sound version of `maybeCompactInboundTools`) and **command-tenuring** (a recurring slash-command promotes to a compact rollup ŌĆö the "store it for aggregate/GC" the goal asked for), and a 4-step first build that wires the goal into the per-turn pin set and measures the effect on the shipped signal/noise metric.
- [vToolcall, forked ŌĆö best-effort serve-tiers and owning the loop (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VTOOLCALL-FORK-AND-BESTEFFORT-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the forward-looking sequel to the materialized-view note, answering "best effort" + "fork an open harness (Crush / opencode)". Adds (1) a **consistency-level QoS axis** (`STRICT | BOUNDED_STALE | BEST_EFFORT | SPECULATIVE` threaded as `c.Meta["consistency"]`, a DB-read-consistency dual) with a per-topology graceful-degradation capability matrix, and (2) the **fork direction**: make fak the tool *dispatcher* so the prior note's "impossible on the proxy" verdict flips and serve-as-ran runs closed-loop on real built-in tools. Honest spine, all three stated plainly: forking does not *soften* un-ring, it **relocates** it (after-consumption ŌåÆ a before-consumption write barrier fak owns, needing a net-new suspend-and-resume turn primitive ŌĆö today's `gateTurn` only terminates); **Crush is FSL-1.1-MIT** (source-available, commercial-restricted at HEAD) so the license-clean call is **build the minimal native TUI on the shipped `internal/agent` seam** (Apache-uniform, single-binary) or host **opencode-as-server** (MIT), never fork Crush into the Apache module; and the loop fak would extend is a **benchmark A/B comparator (`RunArm`) today**, not a production loop. Portfolio verdict: keep the proxy as the universal gate, lead the fork with the native build.
- [vToolcall ŌĆö tool results as a materialized view (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VTOOLCALL-MATERIALIZED-VIEW-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the *closed-loop* dual of [vCache](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VCACHE-VIRTUAL-API-CACHE-2026-06-24.md): serve a tool result from the kernel's own cache **as if the tool ran**, framed as one materialized view in a query engine over the whole harness-facing surface (tool-defs via `promptmmu`, history via `agent.CompactAnthropicHistory`, context via `contextq`, KV via vCache ŌĆö and now results via the vDSO). The serve mechanism already ships as the vDSO FastPath (`kernel.go:347` ŌåÆ `abi.FastPaths().Lookup`); this note is its design rationale with **cache invalidation as the spine**. Honest build boundary: result-synthesis is real only where fak *dispatches* the tool (`fak_syscall`/in-kernel), structurally impossible on the `/v1/messages` proxy (the harness runs tools there); witness-keying of the tier-2 key, a cross-process FILL pool, and the speculative-serve `ProvisionalSink` are named **unbuilt**. Law T (sound-before-serve) is the polarity flip from vCache's A2 ŌĆö a stale tool result *corrupts*, it does not merely *cost*.
- [Fresh status snapshot (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/FRESH-STATUS-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the pinned snapshot of the cross-domain rollup that `tools/fresh_status.py` folds: git context (HEAD/branch/dirty/ahead-behind) + benchmark catalog (run/machine counts, newest-run staleness, per-run **measured | modeled | unknown** provenance with unknown surfaced loudly) + work (plan_audit, zero-state SKIP) + industry parity (`industry_scorecard`), in one `schema/ok/verdict/finding/reason/next_action` control-pane envelope. The sibling of `scorecard_control_pane.py` one level up ŌĆö that folds the scorecard FAMILY, this folds the four top-level domains. Re-generate with `make status` / `python tools/fresh_status.py --write-doc`.
- [What is an agent? - [Long-running agent loops: make fak the loop kernel, not another cron (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/LONG-RUNNING-AGENT-LOOPS-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the operating-system-shaped strategy for rolling cron/launchd/Scheduled Tasks, cloud and remote nodes, phone/Slack/WhatsApp control, notifications, foreground vs background sessions, and green-thread-like logical loops into one fak surface. - [The gardening bundle: default-on the cleanup we run by hand (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/GARDENING-BUNDLE-DEFAULT-ON-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the gardening-workload companion to the loop-kernel note. fak already has three overlapping self-maintenance orchestrators (`scorecard_control_pane` folds 13 scorecards, `fresh_status` folds 4 domains, `fleet_control_pane loop-audit` runs 19 loops) and a built loop runtime (`loopmgr` ledger + governor + `fak loop`), but nothing fires a gardening pass on a cadence and almost nothing auto-fixes. The converge-and-arm plan: one `tools/garden_bundle.py` fold (`make garden`) over the read-only passes, a daily `garden.yml` + a `--mode garden` host tick, the governor as the brake (`FAK_GARDEN=off` / `min_interval_seconds`), then auto-fix behind a `dos commit-audit` witness gate, then a `fak garden` verb. Rungs 0-2 shipped; 3-4 designed.
- [Inbound prompt-MMU: curate WHAT enters the window (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/INBOUND-PROMPT-MMU-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö the INGRESS dual of the result-side `ctxmmu`. fak gates the outbound/result half of the context window end-to-end (quarantine results, adjudicate calls, compact old turns) but the inbound composition ŌĆö tool DEFINITIONS, system, injected skills/memory ŌĆö forwards byte-identical on the `fak guard -- claude` passthrough. The epic + rungs to curate the ingress, on-by-default both directions with flags only for opinionated overrides, all under the #555 cache-prefix byte-splice + fail-safe-identity discipline. Rung 1 (`internal/promptmmu` `CompactInboundTools`, denied-tool-def pruning) SHIPPED; cache geometry forces it to fire at the session-start/RESET prefix rebuild, refusing pre-breakpoint prunes that would bust the session cache.
- [The system-prompt MMU: fak owns its own base context (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SYSTEM-PROMPT-MMU-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö the AUTHORSHIP dual of the inbound prompt-MMU. #751 prunes a harness-authored prompt as it passes through; this epic takes ownership of the *composition* of the head ŌĆö a fak-first base context where the irreducible resident spine is fak's own concepts (the attention-sink anchor, byte-identical every turn) and the harness's rules/skill-menu/environment become a queried, paged, append-after-the-breakpoint overlay assembled from the same capability substrate the skill loader (#1103) already ships. The two end-states: (a) the minimal base where the resident head is *only* fak plus a retrieval affordance, and (b) the live base where the prompt is a first-class object fak rewrites at runtime by page-in+mask under a witness gate. The cache doctrine is REGIME-SCOPED, not universal: "append-and-mask, never edit-in-place" is a hard law only where fak does *not* own the cache (the provider passthrough and the proxy-to-local-engine regimes, whose radix-tree-keyed prefix cache goes cold the moment the head changes ŌĆö Manus' measured ~10├Ś); when fak owns the engine it keeps the pre-RoPE `Kraw` plane and can mutate the head while keeping survivors reusable via **bit-exact span deletion + re-RoPE** (witnessed `max|╬ö|=0`, live behind `FAK_INKERNEL_KVMMU`) ŌĆö though relocated-span *reuse* is not shipped (the repo's own audit faults it to recompute). Grounds the fak-first inversion in attention geometry (regime-independent) plus a cache-stability reason on the passthrough path, states the five load-bearing invariants (spine byte-identical on the provider path; append-and-mask in the provider-uncontrolled regime; safety-critical always resident; pointers-not-bodies with a bounded index; self-modification witness-gated, versioned, append-mostly ŌĆö the agent never rewrites its spine), maps the built-but-request-path-dead substrate this would *wire* rather than reinvent (`cachemeta.PromptSegment`/`promptmmu`/`ctxplan`/`capindex`/`contextq`/`ctxmmu`/`ctxresidency`/`sessionreset` plus the regime-B `model`/`radixkv`/`kvmmu` span machinery), and lays out six rungs from a new `internal/syspromptmmu` spine to the `fak debug` re-derivation surface that feeds #1217. Planning + tickets only.

Records the honest newcomer friction too: `dos --version` is not a flag, `--hooks auto` needs a real agent repo, and the install timing was warm-cache.

- [idea-scout triage: AgenticOS / intent-oriented secure OS (#770) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-agenticos-intent-os-triage-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.21129 (Zhao et al., "AgenticOS: An Intent-Oriented Secure Operating System Architecture for Autonomous AI Agents"): "resource exposure + permission checks" OS security fails once an agent runtime is compromised, because the attacker composes POSIX primitives past the user's task authorization; AgenticOS reframes the OS from a "resource manager" into an "intent filter" ŌĆö agents submit structured **intent declarations** ŌåÆ the system synthesizes a least-privilege env with **mandatory mediation, auditing, and information-flow constraints** ŌĆö via a four-layer kernel (Ghost Kernel, Logic Shutter, Agent Capsule, Semantic Boundary Gateway) + Intent ABI / Manifest-Only Runtime / Weaver capability generation / native-Skill admission. Verdict: **prior art to cite** ŌĆö the cleanest **OS-altitude** statement of fak's founding premise, and its four required properties map onto shipped fak seams that realize them **in userspace** (default-deny capability floor, the one in-process syscall boundary, the witness/decision journal, result-admit quarantine + `wirescreen` + `SinkGate`). Same side of the trust boundary as fak (not a threat). The four-layer **OS-rebuild** architecture + Weaver synthesis are **not adopted** ŌĆö fak's bet is one userspace binary at the tool-call seam ([one-binary-one-surface](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/explainers/one-binary-one-surface.md)), not a new kernel; the honest fence is that an OS-altitude filter mediates a strictly larger primitive set at a strictly larger adoption cost.
- [idea-scout triage: Kamera / position-invariant multimodal KV cache (#583) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-kamera-position-invariant-kv-triage-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.23581 (Ma, Eitzinger, Koestler, Wellein, "Kamera: Unified Position-Invariant Multimodal KV Cache for Training-Free Reuse"): prefix caches serve reuse only at a fixed leading position, so multimodal agents re-encode the same video frames / UI screenshots on every look-back; naive non-prefix KV reuse loses the **cross-chunk conditioning** a chunk absorbs from neighbours, and the loss is **asymmetric** ŌĆö the direct readout is recovered free by the standard **state-merge**, but a diffuse low-rank residue in deep layers remains, invisible to single-hop retrieval yet **halving multi-hop accuracy**; Kamera repairs it with a training-free **low-rank conditioning patch** + one **RoPE re-rotation** operator across MLA/GQA/MHA (recovers full task accuracy on MM-NIAH + 2-page doc-QA, reconstructs KV within bf16 rounding in a production SGLang kernel across six backbones; strongest in vision/video). Verdict: **prior art to cite** ŌĆö the paper fak's own [ON-DEMAND-CONTEXT-KV-REUSE](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ON-DEMAND-CONTEXT-KV-REUSE-2026-06-19.md) note ┬¦2.2/┬¦2.3 anticipated and left open (CacheBlend cross-attention loss + MiniPIC position-independent-but-insufficient), now characterized precisely and given a training-free repair ŌĆö **and a candidate repair** for the conservative RECOMPUTE/REFUSE fault fak's #109 non-prefix-KV audit (`internal/model/nonprefix_kvreuse_audit_test.go`) takes by design. **Adopt only at fak's reserved corrective-path tier (ON-DEMAND ┬¦6 Step 5, at #109 ├Ś the governed vision seam #399) ŌĆö NOT in this increment** (a GPU/kernel epic + it needs a gate fak lacks). **One named invariant:** Kamera's bar is full task accuracy + within-bf16-rounding KV, not fak's bit-exact last-logit; because the loss is asymmetric a patched reuse can pass fak's single-last-logit #109 oracle (a single-hop-shaped probe) while halving multi-hop accuracy ŌĆö so any future patch that promotes a RECOMPUTE fault to a served HIT must clear a **multi-hop** cross-chunk-binding quality probe, never the single-logit check alone. Not a threat (a performance capability on the same side).
- [idea-scout triage: joint intent+harm verification (#910) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-intent-harm-joint-verification-triage-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26377 (Thota, Lei, Thangaraj, Jonnalagadda, Nilizadeh, "Verifying Intent and Harm: A Unified Defense Against LLM-Generated Threats"): prompt-only and response-only defenses miss attacks that exploit the **separation** between adversarial **intent** (in the prompt) and actionable **harm** (only in the response); the paper proposes a verify-before-deliver framework ŌĆö a specialized **Intent Analyst** (scores prompt intent) + a specialized **Harm Analyst** (scores response harm) + a **Judge** that resolves conflicts (reported F1 0.90ŌåÆ0.95, ASR 4.1%, benign-sensitive FPR 0.12ŌåÆ0.06 across jailbreaks/prompt-injection/phishing/cyber-abuse/harmful-content, tested under adaptive attackers who know the verifier). Verdict: **prior art to cite** ŌĆö the join-intent-with-harm thesis is fak's **two-sided adjudication** design at the detector altitude: the kernel already splits the same axis structurally (`k.Decide` folds **only** the pre-call **intent** chain; `k.AdmitResult` is the in-code documented **dual of Decide** and folds the result-side **harm** chain ŌĆö quarantine + IFC taint + `wirescreen`/`SinkGate` egress floor), and the `dos verify` / `dos commit-audit` referee re-checks claimed intent against the actual diff-borne harm. The three-LLM-analyst + Judge **mechanism is NOT adopted** ŌĆö it is a learned detector, and fak's recorded position is **detector-is-not-the-floor** ([defensive-misdirection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-defensive-misdirection-triage-2026-06-23.md), [AUC-is-not-detection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-probe-auc-evaluation-protocol-triage-2026-06-23.md)); the paper's own adaptive-attacker result is the evidence that a learned verifier is evadable where fak's default-deny capability floor the model "can't talk past" is not. **Named residual:** the Harm Analyst scores **content harm in text** (the model emitting phishing/harmful words) that fak's capability gate does not score at the tool-call seam ŌĆö a genuine best-effort-detector rung fak hosts **above** its floor, not on the load-bearing path. Not a threat (a parallel defense on the same side of the boundary).
- [idea-scout triage: ShareLock / multi-tool threshold tool-poisoning against MCP (#911) (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-sharelock-multitool-threshold-poisoning-triage-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.27027 (Liu, Han, Liu, Dong, Ruan, "ShareLock: A Stealthy Multi-Tool Threshold Poisoning Attack Against MCP"): a Tool Poisoning Attack that fragments its malicious instruction with **Shamir's (t, n) threshold secret-sharing** across MULTIPLE innocent-looking MCP tool descriptions, reconstructing the hidden instruction only on a **trigger (a server update)** ŌĆö claiming **information-theoretic** stealth (no description below threshold reveals intent), >90% success, and provable outperformance of single-tool poisoning against **tool-description-based detectors**. Verdict: **prior art to cite + a real threat whose consequence the capability floor already contains** ŌĆö ShareLock attacks the **detection layer** fak deliberately keeps **off the load-bearing path** (`CLAIMS.md` "Honest ceiling": detection is ~100% evadable, **non-load-bearing**), so its core move (disperse-to-evade a description scanner) is **neutralized by construction** against a floor that never reads the descriptions for a verdict and gates the resulting **action** (default-deny capability lock, units 15/29) and **result** (result-admit quarantine, unit 48) instead. ShareLock is the cleanest external **information-theoretic** proof of fak's *detector-is-not-the-floor* thesis, alongside [defensive-misdirection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-defensive-misdirection-triage-2026-06-23.md) / [AUC-is-not-detection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-probe-auc-evaluation-protocol-triage-2026-06-23.md) / [MCPPrivacyDetector](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mcp-privacy-leakage-triage-2026-06-23.md). **Named residual:** fak does not screen inbound third-party tool descriptions, and a poisoned description can still induce a **policy-ALLOWED** call with malicious arguments ŌĆö the standing residual of any capability floor, **contained** (not intercepted) by IFC taint + egress sink-gating. The Shamir-sharing mechanism is **not adopted** (adding a description scanner is the anti-pattern ShareLock refutes); the smallest honest follow-on is to treat a downstream **tool-definition change** as a witnessed provenance event (the trigger fires on server update), filed against the `fak_refute_witness` / `fak_changes` seam. No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` ŌĆö surfaced and scored correctly (topic mcp-security, score 53).
- [idea-scout triage: teaching software engineering with LLM + MCP (#1009) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-llm-mcp-se-education-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.19167 (Chen, Keung, Li, Shao, Li, Ma, "Teaching Software Engineering with LLM and MCP Integration: From Classroom to Industry Practice"): a software-engineering **EDUCATION / pedagogy** study that embeds LLM- and MCP-driven tools into a collaborative **teaching model** (daily teaching, code assistance, engineering simulations) plus industry-internship partnerships, to bridge classroom instruction and industrial workflows. Verdict: **close / not adopted ŌĆö out of kernel scope** ŌĆö fak is a runtime trust + performance gate at the tool-call seam, not a curriculum, teaching platform, or learning framework; the paper proposes **no system fak would adopt, no attack to defend against, and no mechanism-level prior art** for fak's adjudication / cross-turn-reuse design (a clean off-mission sibling of the [PrologMCP capability/security miscategorization](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-prologmcp-symbolic-tool-triage-2026-06-24.md)). **Recorded residual (context, not a citation):** the paper is one more external data point that **MCP is moving into mainstream industrial ŌĆö and now academic ŌĆö SE practice**, the adoption trend that makes a default-deny capability floor on MCP tool calls worth shipping; it is kept as *why-now* context, never cited as prior art for a fak mechanism. **Scout calibration:** surfaced under `mcp-security` (score 47, the lowest of the day's three hits) on a bare `model context protocol` / `mcp (title)` keyword match + freshness ŌĆö a true **on-topic-by-keyword, off-mission-by-content** candidate, the scout working **as designed** (judges new-and-on-topic, never worth-building; hands to human triage). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py`.
- [idea-scout triage: where do CoT training gains land in agents? (#1008) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-cot-training-gains-agents-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26935 (Liu, Wang, Jing, Zhou, Liu, "Where Do CoT Training Gains Land in LLM based Agents?"): an **interpretability / training-analysis** study that asks where CoT-training gains land ŌĆö in **changing the action through generated reasoning** (CoT actions) or in **predicting the action directly from the prompt** with no CoT (prompt actions) ŌĆö and finds, across checkpoints, that **prompt-action quality improves substantially**, i.e. much of the gain lands in the direct promptŌåÆaction mapping rather than in reasoning-driven action change, consistent with prior **CoT-unfaithfulness / post-hoc-rationalization** findings. Verdict: **prior art to cite ŌĆö not adopted, not a threat** ŌĆö fak never reads the model's verbalized reasoning for a verdict and never trusts a self-report; it gates the resulting **action** at the tool-call seam (default-deny capability floor) and re-checks claimed work against the **diff** (`dos verify` / `dos commit-audit`), so this is independent **training-time** evidence for fak's **detector-is-not-the-floor / witness-not-self-report** bet (the action is largely fixed by the prompt before the CoT is written, so a CoT-reading detector audits a post-hoc narrative). It is the **CoT-as-explanation** sibling of the contested **attention-as-explanation** demotion in fak's reward-over-spans epic [#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861)/[#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862) ([witnessed-span-attention-validity](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-witnessed-span-attention-validity-2026-06-26.md)): a self-generated explanation is a **proposal to verify against an action-level witness**, never an accepted causal account. **Recorded residual (NOT adopted):** high prompt-action quality means CoT tokens are non-load-bearing on the easy mass of steps ŌĆö a **token-economy** lever for the `fak dojo` gym ([#951](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/951)) / token-saving-defaults program ŌĆö but only behind a **per-model, per-task accuracy acceptance gate fak lacks**, and the paper itself shows CoT still **changes the action on the hard tail**, so blind CoT-dropping is unsafe and explicitly not shipped. **Scout calibration:** surfaced under `agent-model-arch` (score 50) on genuine `training (title)` / `checkpoint` / `reasoning` terms + freshness ŌĆö correctly on-topic and **closer-to-mission** than the day's #1009 pedagogy hit; the scout working **as designed**. No change to `tools/idea_scout.py`.
- [idea-scout triage: MIRROR / novelty-constrained memory-guided MCTS red-teaming for agentic RAG (#1007) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mirror-novelty-mcts-redteam-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26793 (Singh, Murillo, Sekiya, Unno, Suga, "MIRROR: Novelty-Constrained Memory-Guided MCTS Red-Teaming for Agentic RAG"): an **offensive cross-surface attack GENERATOR** for multimodal agentic RAG ŌĆö memory-guided Monte Carlo tree search that conditions candidate generation on retrieved context under a **deterministic Novelty Gate** (rejects any candidate matching the retrieval set under normalized comparison) across **text poisoning / image injection / direct-query / orchestrator-level tool manipulation**, motivated by measuring **73-84% exact template duplication** in existing surface-specific red-teaming. Verdict (three-part, the shape of [#909](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-out-of-band-injection-defense-taxonomy-triage-2026-06-26.md) / [#911](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-sharelock-multitool-threshold-poisoning-triage-2026-06-26.md)): **(1) prior art to cite** ŌĆö MIRROR is the **SOTA academic form of fak's own dynamic red-team** (`internal/agentdojo`'s `{plain,obfuscated,paraphrased}` adaptivity axis + `examples/agentdojo-redteam`'s marker-free generative expander) and the **named answer** to the #909 follow-on (make fak's AgentDojo-style ASR battery adaptive, defense-aware, and **cross-surface**, not a static injection set); its deterministic Novelty Gate is the formal sibling of fak's own `tools/idea_scout.py` dedup/novelty rungs (seen-cache + Jaccard near-dup rejection). **(2) A real threat whose CONSEQUENCE the structural floor already contains** ŌĆö MIRROR's entire edge is **novelty engineered to evade template/pattern detectors**, but fak's full-stack defense taints by **PROVENANCE not content**, so the egress/destructive sink is barred *"regardless of phrasing"* (`internal/agentdojo/agentdojo.go`: ASR(full-stack)==0 vs ASR(detection-only)>0) ŌĆö the novelty axis is **invariant against the floor by construction**, the same outcome as [ShareLock #911](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-sharelock-multitool-threshold-poisoning-triage-2026-06-26.md); the novelty advantage lands entirely on the detection-only layer fak keeps **off the load-bearing path** (~100% evadable, by design). **Named residual:** containment is not a *measured number* ŌĆö fak has no adaptive cross-surface ASR-under-containment metric, and the **image-injection** (#290, `internal/model/multimodal`, fail-closed `MultimodalModeQuarantine`) / **orchestrator** (gateway/MCP, adjudicated tool call) surfaces are governed but unmeasured against an adaptive generator. **(3) Mechanism NOT adopted** ŌĆö fak is a defense; the one-binary zero-dep kernel does not generate attacks (no MCTS / memory / LLM-conditioned generator on the request path; `OUT_OF_DIRECTION` would refuse it). The smallest honest follow-on is the **evaluation upgrade** (expand `internal/agentdojo` toward a novelty-constrained cross-surface generator + add the image/orchestrator surfaces, turning the standing `docs/industry-scorecard/security.md` "no measured adaptive ASR" fence into a tracked agentdojo-lane task), filed not built ŌĆö with the fence that MIRROR is cited as a **methodology + a contained threat**, never as evidence fak's containment is adaptively robust. No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` ŌĆö surfaced and scored correctly (topic `prompt-injection-defense`, score 50).
- [idea-scout triage: how reliable is your jailbreak judge? / ASR-judge calibration + adversarial robustness (#1097) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-jailbreak-judge-asr-reliability-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.25487 (Yang Gao, "How Reliable Is Your Jailbreak Judge? Calibration and Adversarial Robustness of Automated ASR Scoring"): a **measurement-validity** study that checks the automated **judge** assigning nearly every reported attack-success-rate (ASR) ŌĆö a safety classifier or a prompted chat model ŌĆö against **596 human-labeled** HarmBench completions, then attacks it. The two families fail in opposite ways: the dedicated classifier **over-flags** (P **0.835**, R **0.974**); three LLM-as-judges keep high precision (**0.81-0.94**) but **erratic recall (0.06-0.65)**, so the same responses yield very different ASR by judge. **Benign framing wrappers** that leave the harmful text intact flip **every LLM-judge 57-100%** (a single prepended refusal sentence = **39-88%**); the classifier resists surface attacks (**Ōēż6.7%**) but a small-budget **white-box GCG** on its open weights flips **70%** of its confident true positives (21/30) ŌĆö an 80-sample audit confirms the flips left the harm intact. Verdict: **prior art to cite ŌĆö not adopted, not a threat** ŌĆö it is measured, external evidence for fak's **detector-is-not-the-floor / witness-not-self-report** thesis and a direct validation of fak's **choice of success criterion**: fak's own ASR (`asr_fullstack=0` = 0/38, `asr_detection=0.763` = 29/38, `benign_completion_rate=1`) is assigned by **no judge model at all** ŌĆö `internal/agentdojo/agentdojo.go` computes `succeeded := injectionReachedContext && sinkExecuted` from deterministic adjudicator verdicts (`VerdictQuarantine` / `VerdictDeny`; the code comment reads *"Deterministic, in-process, no model"*), `catch_reasons` drawn from the closed structural vocabulary (`TRUST_VIOLATION` 29 / `MALFORMED` 9). Because there is no judge model in fak's success decision, the three attack families the paper documents (framing wrappers, prepended-refusal, GCG-on-judge-weights) are **out of fak's metric by construction** ŌĆö you cannot frame your way past a `VerdictDeny` that gates **PROVENANCE** (taintedŌåÆsink), not content (same shape as [MIRROR #1007](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mirror-novelty-mcts-redteam-triage-2026-06-27.md) / [ShareLock #911](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-sharelock-multitool-threshold-poisoning-triage-2026-06-26.md)). The metric-side twin of [AUC-is-not-detection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-probe-auc-evaluation-protocol-triage-2026-06-23.md) / [defensive-misdirection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-defensive-misdirection-triage-2026-06-23.md) and the [FRONTIER survey](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/FRONTIER-AGENT-LEADERBOARD-SURVEY-2026-06.md) HAL *"benchmarks gameable to near-perfect without solving tasks"* anchor. **Honest fence (carried forward):** the immunity is specific to fak's **structural flow-reachability witness** ŌĆö the day fak reports a **judge-mediated** ASR (a content-harm probe, or any external benchmark whose ASR is LLM-graded and that fak compares against), it must adopt the paper's protocol: report **judge precision/recall on a human-labeled slice**, report **ASR corrected for judge precision**, and include an **adversarial check of the judge**. **Mechanism not adopted** (a measurement-validity result, no kernel surface). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` ŌĆö surfaced and scored correctly (topic `prompt-injection-defense`, score 47).
- [idea-scout triage: data-centric survey of privacy in LLM agents (#1098) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-agent-privacy-data-centric-survey-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26627 (Lahjouji, Colaco, "Agents That Know Too Much: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents"): a **survey** that organizes LLM-agent privacy around the **data an agent touches** ŌĆö taxonomizing the data sources (databases, document collections, external APIs, agent memory, cross-session state), the privacy risk each creates (**query leakage, intermediate-result exposure, memory-write vulnerabilities, inter-agent messaging**), and the **governance mechanisms** that address them (it names **information-flow control** + access control), then maps the benchmarks and identifies what is missing. Verdict: **prior art to cite + a real multi-channel threat fak's own IFC governance gates UNEVENLY by provenance** ŌĆö fak ships one of the two governance families the survey names (`internal/ifc`: a provenance taint `Ledger` (`SourceTaint`) ŌåÆ a `SinkClass` (Egress/Exec/Destructive) ŌåÆ `Policy.Gates` refusing a taintedŌåÆgated-sink flow, `DefaultGatedSinks`=EGRESS+DESTRUCTIVE, CaMeL-style `Authorize` escape), so the **final-answer/egress** sink and the **secret-shaped intermediate-result** channel (the [#552 result-admit gate](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mcp-privacy-leakage-triage-2026-06-23.md)) are **gated-by-provenance**; but the survey's **query-issuance** channel is gated-by-capability not by a privacy assessment of the query content, **memory-write** rides the [memory-view contract](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/MEMORY-VIEW-CONTRACT-2026-06-26.md) read-side only, and **inter-agent messages** cross a boundary the session-scoped taint ledger does not provably propagate across ŌĆö all **gated-by-capability-only or unmeasured** ŌĆö and fak reports **no measured privacy-leakage number** (the same *containment-is-not-a-measured-number* residual as [MIRROR #1007](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mirror-novelty-mcts-redteam-triage-2026-06-27.md), now stated for the privacy axis). **Mechanism NOT adopted** (a survey, no kernel component); the smallest honest follow-on is a **per-channel privacy-posture audit** (the survey's data-source x risk grid ŌåÆ each channel labeled gated-by-provenance / gated-by-capability-only / unmeasured) + a **candidate privacy-leakage metric**, FILED not built ŌĆö with the fence the survey is cited as field framing + a coverage map, never as evidence fak's privacy containment is complete or measured. The field-level umbrella over the source-specific [MCPPrivacyDetector triage (#552)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mcp-privacy-leakage-triage-2026-06-23.md), which instantiates one cell (MCP-server returns). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` ŌĆö surfaced and scored correctly (topic `prompt-injection-defense`, score 47).
- [idea-scout triage: PRCR / position-rebinding cache reuse for replay-free visual revisiting (#1099) (2026-06-27)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-position-rebinding-cache-reuse-triage-2026-06-27.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26631 (Wang, Ji, Zuo, Ye, Tu, "Position Rebinding Cache Reuse: Replay-Free Visual Revisiting for Interleaved Multimodal Reasoning"): interleaved multimodal reasoning revisits visual evidence during multi-step generation and pays for it with **token replay** (re-forwarding selected visual tokens); the shortcut ŌĆö reusing the historical visual KV cache directly ŌĆö has a critical failure mode, **cached visual keys are bound to their ORIGINAL positional context**, so reuse under a later decoding context distorts attention and can trigger **SEVERE AUTOREGRESSIVE DECODING COLLAPSE**; PRCR's fix is **position rebinding** (store raw visual KV with original coords, reassign position-compatible coords to selected entries, rebind keys before injecting into the active decoder cache ŌĆö preserving textual positional continuity + relative visual structure), reporting replay-level-or-better quality, **+5%** average accuracy, and up to **tens-of-thousands├Ś** less visual-revisiting compute. Verdict: **prior art to cite ŌĆö the collapse-naming sibling of [Kamera #583](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-kamera-position-invariant-kv-triage-2026-06-26.md)** and the second hit in the non-prefix / position-aware visual-KV-reuse neighbourhood fak's own [ON-DEMAND-CONTEXT-KV-REUSE](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ON-DEMAND-CONTEXT-KV-REUSE-2026-06-19.md) note ┬¦2.2/┬¦2.3 anticipated and its **#109** non-prefix-KV audit (`internal/model/nonprefix_kvreuse_audit_test.go`) proves locally. Where Kamera characterized the loss as a diffuse residue that **halves multi-hop accuracy**, PRCR names the **worst case** (an outright collapsed decode) ŌĆö the strongest external justification yet for fak's **#109 RECOMPUTE/REFUSE** conservative default (*"a failed exactness budget must produce RECOMPUTE/REFUSE, never HIT"*). The sharp insight: fak's shipped EXACT reuse is **prefix-only**, where cached tokens occupy the **same** leading positions on reuse, so PRCR's failure mode **cannot arise on fak's exact surface by construction** ŌĆö it appears only at the non-prefix splice the #109 audit refuses; PRCR is independent evidence fak's **prefix-only-exact HIT + non-prefix-faults-to-recompute** structure is the safe one. **Adopt the rebinding operator only at fak's reserved corrective-path tier** (ON-DEMAND ┬¦6 Step 5, at #109 ├Ś the governed vision seam `internal/model/multimodal.go` #399, fail-closed `MultimodalModeQuarantine`) ŌĆö **NOT in this increment** (a GPU/kernel epic + it needs a quality gate fak lacks). **One named invariant** (carried from the Kamera triage and tightened): a rebinding patch that promotes a RECOMPUTE fault to a served HIT must clear a **multi-hop** cross-chunk quality probe AND a **no-collapse** check ŌĆö fak's bit-exact-last-logit-or-fault gate already enforces the no-collapse half for free (a within-rounding rebind keeps faulting), so the binding precondition for USING a rebind is the multi-hop probe fak still lacks. Not a threat (a performance capability on the same side). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` ŌĆö surfaced and scored correctly (topic `kv-prefix-cache-reuse`, score 47).
- [idea-scout triage: progress advantage ŌĆö a free RL-post-training step reward for LLM agents (#1122) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-progress-advantage-rl-posttraining-triage-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26080 (Oh, Li, Park, Yeh, Mallick, Li, "Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents"): process reward models give step-level LLM evaluation but are prohibitively hard to build for **agentic** settings (long-horizon, irreversible actions, stochastic feedback defeat annotation + Monte-Carlo at scale); the paper shows **RL post-training already contains the step-level signal** ŌĆö under a general stochastic MDP the **log-prob ratio between the RL-trained policy and its reference policy exactly recovers the optimal advantage** (termed **progress advantage**: annotation-free, domain-agnostic, a free byproduct of the standard post-training pipeline), validated on test-time scaling / uncertainty quantification / failure attribution across 5 benchmarks + 4 model families, beating confidence baselines and **surpassing dedicated trained reward models**. Verdict: **prior art to cite ŌĆö at the thesis level, with a sharp boundary** ŌĆö the paper's "the existing pipeline already contains the step-level signal, so train no dedicated judge" is the **reward-modeling-side sibling** of fak's witness referee (`dos verify` / `dos commit-audit` grade a claim against the **diff git actually recorded** ŌĆö a derived, annotation-free witness ŌĆö not a trained reward model or the worker's self-report), and the empirical "free signal beats trained PRMs" is independent support for that **don't-build-the-judge** bet. **Sharp fence:** progress advantage is the policy's **own log-probability** (benchmarked against **confidence-based** baselines), a **model-internal self-report**; fak's recorded **detector-is-not-the-floor / witness-not-self-report** stance ([defensive-misdirection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-defensive-misdirection-triage-2026-06-23.md), [AUC-is-not-detection](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-probe-auc-evaluation-protocol-triage-2026-06-23.md)) keeps a signal read off the model's own probabilities **above** the trust floor, never **on** it ŌĆö the **policy-log-prob sibling** of the CoT-as-explanation demotion in the [#1008 CoT-training triage](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-cot-training-gains-agents-triage-2026-06-27.md) and the attention-as-explanation demotion in [#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861)/[#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862) (a self-generated signal is a **proposal verified against an action-level witness**, never an accepted causal account). **Not adopted as a capability** ŌĆö fak does not RL-post-train, and the "free lunch" needs paired RL-policy + reference-policy log-probs for the SAME served model that **fak's third-party/local fleet has no reference policy for**, so the signal is **not even computable** on fak's models; no kernel surface (same off-mission shape as #1008 / [#1098 privacy survey](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-agent-privacy-data-centric-survey-triage-2026-06-27.md)). **Not a threat.** **One recorded residual, NOT adopted:** progress advantage as a **cheap proposal signal** for fak's trajectory graders (the paper's **failure-attribution** application maps onto fak's failure-to-witness need ŌĆö fleet closure-honesty + the `fak dojo` predictŌåÆrunŌåÆmeasureŌåÆevalŌåÆcalibrate gym [#951](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/951)), gated behind a paired-policy availability fak lacks and fenced as **never-the-floor**. **Scout calibration:** surfaced under `agent-model-arch` (score 44) on genuine `training (title)` + freshness ŌĆö correctly on-topic; the **third** recent training-time `agent-model-arch` hit fak cannot adopt as a capability (after #1008; cf. #861/#862), an on-topic-by-keyword/off-mission-by-content pattern worth watching but **not** a scoring bug (these still yield citable thesis-level prior art). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py`.
- [idea-scout triage: Tool Suppression under joint tool-call + JSON-schema constraints (#1121) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-tool-suppression-constraint-tax-triage-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.25605 (Li, Zhang, Lv, "Constraint Tax in Open-Weight LLMs: An Empirical Study of Tool Calling Suppression Under Structured Output Constraints"): a **serving-side** result ŌĆö when Tool Calling and a JSON-Schema output constraint are enabled **jointly**, multiple **open-weight** model families **stop invoking tools** while keeping **high schema compliance**, a phenomenon it names **Tool Suppression** (a **"Constraint Tax"** the structured-output constraint silently levies on the tool-calling capability), reproduced across model families/settings; each capability is functional **in isolation**. Verdict: **prior art to cite + empirical validation of fak's [#907](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/907) "one canonical internal shape" decision** ŌĆö fak models tool-CALLING **as** the schema (`grammar.Grammar` ŌåÆ a `oneOf`-of-tools JSON Schema ŌåÆ the `response_format` carrier OR a native logit mask [#929]), so the **two-competing-constraints** configuration that produces Tool Suppression (an independent output `response_format` stacked on a tool-calling turn) is exactly the one fak's unified shape **avoids**; the paper strengthens #907/#929 rather than asking fak to build anything. **A self-inflicted failure mode fak can induce two ways:** the native guided-decode mask (`internal/model/constraint.go`, behind `FAK_NATIVE_GUIDED_DECODE=1`, which **defaults OFF** ŌĆö a schema mask is never stacked on a tool-calling turn unless an operator opts in, an accidental protection), and the [#907 passthrough](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-structured-output-decoding-2026-06-26.md) (`internal/gateway/http.go:464`) forwarding a client `response_format` to an upstream vLLM/SGLang alongside tools, where any suppression is the **upstream's** behavior ŌĆö **OBSERVED/relayed, not a fak action** (the controlled-vs-observed conflation boundary). **The load-bearing trust-floor point (why it is labelled `trust-floor`):** a suppressed tool call is **invisible** to fak's default-deny adjudication floor ŌĆö `internal/adjudicator/decide.go` `Adjudicate(ctx, *abi.ToolCall)` only ever receives a call that **was** emitted, so Tool Suppression is a capability **false-negative below the floor** (no call = nothing to deny = trivially "safe" to a deny-bad-calls gate) that an adjudicator **structurally cannot** catch by adjudicating calls; detection requires **observing the absence** (tool offered + joint schema active + zero calls + schema-valid prose), an **observability** ask fak does not measure today. **Not adopted** as a new mechanism (the right design ships as #907); **not** a classic threat (a constraint-interaction defect, no adversary). **One residual, FILED not built:** a gateway "tool-suppression-suspected" observability counter, gated behind a **served-open-weight reproduction this win32 dev box cannot run** (the "expected a tool call" half needs calibration ŌĆö shipping it now would be an unwitnessed "expected" claim). No capability code change; no change to `tools/idea_scout.py` (surfaced + scored correctly under topic `tool-call-adjudication`, score 44, on-topic and close-to-mission).
- [idea-scout triage: EpiKV ŌĆö KV eviction by the epiphany score, no attention matrix (#1123) (2026-06-28)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-epikv-attention-free-eviction-triage-2026-06-28.md) ŌĆö triage of arXiv:2606.26472 (Kolawole, Smith, "Epiphany-Aware KV Cache Eviction Without the Attention Matrix" / EpiKV): a **serving-side** eviction **policy** ŌĆö existing methods rank tokens by **attention weight**, which the paper argues is a noisy importance proxy in long reasoning traces AND **prohibits fused kernels** by forcing the model to materialize the attention matrix; EpiKV instead scores tokens by the **epiphany score** (the change in the model's internal representation, read from the forward pass with no attention matrix), training-free and custom-kernel-free. Verdict: **prior art to cite + validation of fak's attention-as-explanation demotion ([#861](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/861)/[#862](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/862)) in the EVICTION-POLICY domain + a candidate fused-kernel-friendly importance POLICY for fak's policy-agnostic exact-eviction MECHANISM**. fak's value-add is the bit-exact mid-run evictor (`internal/model/kvcache.go` `Evict`, the paged twin `internal/model/paged_evict.go` `PagedKV.Evict` [#33], the `internal/kvmmu/` governance evictor) ŌĆö it evicts a span handed to it and leaves the cache byte-for-byte `== never-saw-it` (`max|╬ö|=0`), so the importance scorer is a **swappable input**; fak already shipped ONE policy ŌĆö [#856](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/issues/856) "attention-informed exact eviction ŌĆö evict coldest-by-attention via the existing bit-exact evictor" (CLOSED, kvmmu lane), fed by the post-softmax attention mass `internal/model/attn_observer.go` emits. **The load-bearing fak-specific point:** #856's attention mass is materialized for free on fak's CPU softmax seam, but a **fused GPU attention kernel** (FlashAttention/FlashInfer ŌĆö fak's production decode direction) does NOT materialize the attention matrix, so carrying coldest-by-attention onto the fused path forces materializing it ŌĆö the exact tax EpiKV names ŌĆö whereas the epiphany score (a hidden-state delta) is forward-pass-readable without it; EpiKV is the fused-kernel-compatible importance signal for the gap #856 leaves on fak's fused path. **NOT adopted as a mechanism:** fak has an attention-mass observer but NO hidden-state-delta observer (the epiphany score's carrier), and proving any lossy eviction policy preserves answer quality on long reasoning traces needs a **served long-CoT eviction-quality benchmark this win32 dev box cannot run** (host-gated, [AVOID-TESTING-ON-THIS-MACHINE](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/AVOID-TESTING-ON-THIS-MACHINE-2026-06-25.md)). **Not a threat** (a performance capability, no adversary). **One residual, FILED not built:** a forward-pass hidden-state-delta observer feeding the kvmmu exact evictor (the representation-delta twin of `attn_observer`ŌåÆ#856), gated on that served witness. The one concrete artifact: a ┬¦B2 catalog row in [`docs/awesome-token-efficiency.md`](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/awesome-token-efficiency.md). No capability code change; no change to `tools/idea_scout.py` (surfaced + scored correctly under topic `kv-prefix-cache-reuse`, score 44, on-topic and close-to-mission).
- [idea-scout triage: Pipelock / agent egress firewall with signed action receipts (#1267) (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-pipelock-agent-egress-receipts-triage-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö triage of GitHub repo `luckyPipewrench/pipelock`: a Go agent-egress firewall that mediates HTTP/WebSocket/CONNECT/MCP/A2A traffic, scans for secret exfiltration / SSRF / prompt injection, and emits mediator-signed action receipts. Verdict: **prior art to cite + same-side sibling control** ŌĆö it independently validates fak's mediator-outside-the-agent boundary and witness-not-self-report stance, especially external evidence from the mediator; scanner/proxy mechanisms are **not adopted** because fak's load-bearing floor remains structured tool-call adjudication, IFC sink gating, and result admission, with detectors deliberately additive. **Named residual:** fak's audit journal is hash-chained and tamper-evident, but not a pinned-signer action-receipt format; signed receipt export is a future audit-evidence follow-on, not shipped here. No change to `tools/idea_scout.py`.
- [idea-scout triage: FuzzingLabs/mcp-security-hub ŌĆö offensive-security MCP tooling (#1265) (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mcp-security-hub-offensive-tooling-triage-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö triage of GitHub repo `FuzzingLabs/mcp-security-hub` (595 stars, MIT): a catalog of **38 Dockerized MCP servers across 14 security domains** that expose offensive-security tools to AI assistants ŌĆö recon (Nmap, Masscan, Shodan), web exploitation (Nuclei, SQLMap, Nikto, Ffuf, Burp), binary RE (Radare2, Ghidra, IDA Pro), credential cracking (Hashcat), AD pathing (BloodHound), exploit lookup (Searchsploit), each wired in through `claude_desktop_config.json`. Verdict: **a concrete dual-use THREAT-SURFACE EXEMPLAR + prior art to cite; mechanism NOT adopted** ŌĆö it is the first idea-scout candidate that is a real, popular, attach-in-minutes inventory of high-capability offensive tools rather than a paper, i.e. exactly the tool inventory fak's **default-deny capability floor** exists to govern. Each tool reaches fak as a structured tool call; none matches the guard default policy's allowlist or its read-only allow-prefixes (`read_`/`get_`/`search_`/`list_`/`lookup_`/`find_`), so each stays **`DEFAULT_DENY`** at the kernel (`cmd/fak/attest.go:237`) until an operator authorizes it ŌĆö the **enforced** form of the repo's own README process (obtain written authorization, define scope, maintain audit logs), turned from prose into a capability policy + a hash-chained decision journal, with offensive calls further mapping onto fak's IFC sink classes (Exec/Egress/Destructive). **Honest fence:** fak denies these because they are not allow-listed, **not** because it "knows" Nmap is offensive ([detector-is-not-the-floor](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-defensive-misdirection-triage-2026-06-23.md)) ŌĆö so fak ships **no** curated offensive-tooling starter policy and **no** adjudicator test corpus drawn from this catalog; the smallest honest follow-on is that fixture/corpus, **FILED not built**. **Adopt?** No ŌĆö fak is the governor, not the offensive toolbox. **Defend against?** Indirectly ŌĆö the threat is an agent with these tools attached and invoked without authorization/scope; the default-deny floor + journal + sink-gating is the containment. **Cite as prior art?** Yes, as a dual-use threat-surface catalog, never as a fak mechanism ŌĆö the offensive-MCP sibling of the defensive-MCP triages [#552](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-mcp-privacy-leakage-triage-2026-06-23.md) / [#911](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-sharelock-multitool-threshold-poisoning-triage-2026-06-26.md) / [#1267](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-pipelock-agent-egress-receipts-triage-2026-06-29.md). No change to `tools/idea_scout.py` (surfaced + scored correctly, topic `mcp-security`, score 40).
- [Ultra-long-context: levels, levers, naming (2026-06-22)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/RESEARCH-ultra-long-context-levels-and-naming-2026-06-22.md)
- [Scaling laws of agents (2026-06-19)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SCALING-LAWS-OF-AGENTS-2026-06-19.md)
- [Throughput parity and the trust/L3 moat share one spine (2026-06-24)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/THROUGHPUT-TRUST-SHARED-SPINE-2026-06-24.md) ŌĆö connecting tissue between the throughput (dual-track serving) and trust/L3-disaggregation tracks: the bottom half of both roadmaps is the same code (lifecycle seam, KV-span byte form + mover, paged KV, continuous-batching scheduler). The one load-bearing tension ŌĆö bit-exact `Evict` needs owned `Kraw` rows so it dies on a ridden engine but survives over a fak-governed L3 tier ŌĆö and a worst-regret-minimizing sequence that front-loads the shared spine then forks hardware-ungated (trust) from hardware-gated (throughput). Every load-bearing claim adversarially verified; epic #637 + seam #638.
- [Capability floor, a security note (2026-06-18)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SECURITY-capability-floor-2026-06-18.md)

Satisfies #399's *Blocked By: security review of image handling* and the Phase 4 security-review DoD item.
- [Session control state as a first-class value (2026-06-24)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/SESSION-CONTROL-STATE-AS-FIRST-CLASS-2026-06-24.md) ŌĆö make a served session's DRIVE state (run-state, planner budget, priority, pace) a TraceID-keyed value read each turn and written live, instead of re-derived from git commits + a 0-byte-log scan. Generalizes the existing `ifc.Ledger` + `/v1/fak/trace` seam, which already proves the live-mutable per-session pattern for one bit (taint), from a bit to a small struct. Design + honest fences; nothing shipped.
- [Portable session images + a uniform dump/restore seam (2026-06-24)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/PORTABLE-SESSION-IMAGE-AND-SNAPSHOT-2026-06-24.md) ŌĆö make a session ŌĆö and any primitive on the loops ladder (turn, tool, session, fleet, RSI) ŌĆö a first-class value you can dump, archive, offload, and RESUME across hosts, users, instances, VMs, and a model change. `session.Table.Restore` (durable drive re-attach) + `internal/sessionimage` (a model-agnostic, sha256-integrity bundle + `.faksession` tar that keeps the recall quarantine moat across the offload boundary) + `internal/snapshot` (the uniform envelope over any primitive). SHIPPED; closes the SESSION-CONTROL ┬¦5 persistence fence.
- [Sound restore against semantic rollback replay (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ACRFENCE-SOUND-RESTORE-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö runnable fixture: a restored session consults a `VerifiedDone` witness and does not re-fire an already completed irreversible effect, while an ungated ACRFence-style semantic-rollback control fires the regenerated effect twice. Honest scope: fixture, not a transactional effects store.
- [The account switcher's single-source problem, and why gem8 "disappeared" (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ACCOUNT-SWITCHER-SINGLE-SOURCE-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö diagnosis + consolidation path: `claude-accounts observe` (the `job` repo) reads a different roster than `dos accounts` and `fak accounts`, so gem8 reads "tombstoned" in one and "active" in another; and a config dir has TWO credentials (interactive login vs setup `.oauth-token`) that can name different accounts, so one rate-limit bucket shows as several "serving" seats. Shipped: `internal/accounts` now fingerprints the setup token (one-way), reconciles same-account seats to one canonical, flags token-twins, and de-noises `NameLie` ŌĆö `fak accounts list` collapses 13 host seats to 8 real accounts.
- [Account-lifecycle runbook ŌĆö enroll, serve, retire, purge (2026-06-26)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/ACCOUNT-LIFECYCLE-RUNBOOK-2026-06-26.md) ŌĆö the durable, super-easy procedure for adding/retiring a Claude config-home seat plus a keep-updating lessons log: registry is the single source, the dos + job (`u`) rosters are generated views. Add is one command (`fak accounts add <name>`); retire is one command (`fak accounts remove --name <seat> --archive` ŌĆö tombstone + `.DELETED-<date>` dir-rename + registry repoint + resync, refusing the live `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` seat). Records the 2026-06-26 four-seat retirement and its traps (a stale `fak.exe` fails silently on a new verb; you cannot retire your own seat; check `~/.claude-account-backups` before a hard delete; `remove` synced only the dos view until `FAK_JOB_ROSTER` was exported), and the shipped versioning surface (`fak accounts version` + a `list` provenance header) that makes a stale binary visible.
- [Avoid testing/serving on this dev box for now (2026-06-25)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/AVOID-TESTING-ON-THIS-MACHINE-2026-06-25.md) ŌĆö operational note: native `go test` is OS-blocked on this Windows box (use WSL `./test.ps1` or CI), and long-lived local `fak serve` leftovers pile up memory on a shared multi-session tree (a stray Vulkan serve held 4.3 GB idle). Use the GPU/cloud nodes for a real serve; kill local serves when done.
- [GLM-5.2 on a CPU-only box (CPU server): the llama.cpp pure-CPU baseline + the fak-native RAM wall (2026-06-29)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/GLM52-CPU-LLAMACPP-BASELINE-2026-06-29.md) ŌĆö witnessed pp512=16.1 / tg64=1.47 tok/s for the real 753B GLM-5.2 q4 on a CPU-only EPYC 7742 (AVX2) via llama.cpp mmap; the sequential-prewarm + plain-mmap levers that made it measurable (cold random mmap-faults at 0.09 GB/s vs warm); and the dmesg-witnessed reason fak-native is still RAM-gated here (q4 all-resident ŌåÆ FitTooBig; q3/IQ3_XXS dequants to f32 ŌåÆ OOM-kill at ~464 GB anon).

- [Permission-systems visuals (2026-06-18)](https://github.com/anthony-chaudhary/fak/blob/main/docs/notes/VISUALS-permission-systems-2026-06-18.md)
